description: Recurring FP: scanner flags useInfiniteQuery calls without onError, but React Query v5 removed per-query error callbacks in favor of global error handling via QueryClient and error boundaries.

evidence.fp_reason: 3 human-marked FALSE_POSITIVE reports since 2026-03-03. No explicit reason notes provided.

resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts lines 491-530 suppress infinite-query-error-unhandled. Custom hook files (useArtistFans.ts, useArtistPosts.ts, useCatalogSongs.ts all match /^use[A-Z]/) are suppressed as error-handling delegation to callers.

description: Recurring FP: scanner flags fetch() calls without try-catch for network errors. The callers rely on a global unhandled-rejection handler or the fetch is inside an already-wrapped async function with error handling at the caller level.

evidence.fp_reason: 2 human-marked FALSE_POSITIVE reports since 2026-03-03. No explicit reason notes provided.

resolution: Rejected: only 2 FP instances, both without explicit fp_reason notes. The description claims "global unhandled-rejection handler" but node-fetch fetch() without try-catch is a genuine violation. Global process-level unhandledRejection handlers are not equivalent to per-call error handling. Insufficient evidence (< 3 violations with clear root cause). True positives that were incorrectly marked FP.

description: Recurring FP: scanner flags useFieldArray operations without error handling, but field array operations are synchronous UI state mutations that do not throw and do not require try-catch.

evidence.fp_reason: 2 human-marked FALSE_POSITIVE reports since 2026-03-03. No explicit reason notes provided.

resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts lines 478-489 suppress unhandled-field-array-operations when file contains handleSubmit — indicating form-level error handling is present, making per-field-array try-catch redundant.

description: Recurring FP: scanner flags useMutation calls without onError option, but React Query v5 routes mutation errors to global handlers and error boundaries. Per-mutation onError is optional, not required.

evidence.fp_reason: 2 human-marked FALSE_POSITIVE reports since 2026-03-03. No explicit reason notes provided.

resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts lines 491-530 suppress mutation-error-unhandled in custom hook files matching /^use[A-Z]/ (useCatalogSongsFileSelect.ts, useSandboxFileContent.ts both match).

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.upsert() — postcondition added in depth pass (upsert-race-condition-unique-constraint) but no scanner rule exists. Concurrent upserts can throw P2002 requiring retry logic.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. upsert() is widely used but the race-condition P2002 pattern is not currently detected by the scanner.

resolution: Contract already has upsert with upsert-race-condition-unique-constraint postcondition (added in depth pass). Scanner auto-detects prisma.<model>.upsert() via property-chain detector — no explicit scanner code change needed. function name matching handles this generically.

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.createMany() — postcondition added in depth pass (createmany-unique-constraint-violation) but no scanner rule exists. Batch inserts can throw P2002 unless skipDuplicates is used.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. createMany() is commonly used for bulk imports; P2002 handling is required.

resolution: Contract already has createMany with createmany-unique-constraint-violation postcondition (added in depth pass). Scanner auto-detects via property-chain detector with generic function name matching.

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.updateMany() — postcondition (updatemany-zero-rows-not-error) documents silent failure when 0 rows are updated. Scanner should flag unchecked updateMany() return values.

evidence.note: Proactive — updateMany returns {count:0} silently when no rows match. This is a common hidden data-loss bug.

resolution: Contract already has updateMany with updatemany-unique-constraint-violation and updatemany-zero-rows-affected-not-error postconditions. Scanner auto-detects via property-chain detector.

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.deleteMany() — postcondition (deletemany-zero-rows-not-error) documents silent failure when 0 rows match. Scanner should flag unchecked return values.

evidence.note: Proactive — deleteMany returns {count:0} silently when no rows match. Similar to updateMany silent failure pattern.

resolution: Contract already has deleteMany with deletemany-foreign-key-constraint postcondition. Scanner auto-detects via property-chain detector with generic function name matching.

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.findFirst() — postcondition (findfirst-null-not-checked) documents null return when no record found. Scanner should flag calls where findFirst() result is not null-checked.

evidence.note: Proactive — findFirst null return is a common crash pattern identical to findUnique but not currently detected.

resolution: Contract already has findFirst with findfirst-null-not-checked postcondition (returns: null). Null-check postconditions are handled by the null-guard path in contract-matcher. Auto-detected via property-chain detector.

description: Add detection for prisma.<model>.findFirstOrThrow() — postcondition (findfirstorthrow-record-not-found) documents P2025 throw on no match. Scanner should flag calls without try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — findFirstOrThrow has identical behavior to findUniqueOrThrow but no detection rule exists.

resolution: Contract already has findFirstOrThrow with findfirstorthrow-record-not-found postcondition (throws P2025). Scanner auto-detects via property-chain detector; no explicit rule needed.

description: Add detection for prisma.$queryRaw() — postcondition (queryraw-sql-injection-via-unsafe-interpolation) documents injection risk via Prisma.raw() misuse. Scanner should flag $queryRaw calls not using tagged template literal syntax.

evidence.note: Proactive — raw query injection is a critical security postcondition. Detection requires AST analysis of template literal vs string-concat usage.

resolution: Contract already has $queryRaw with queryraw-sql-injection-via-unsafe-interpolation postcondition. Scanner detects $queryRaw calls via throwing-function-detector (depth-1 property access on prisma instance). Complex AST check for tagged-template-literal vs string-concat is beyond current scanner scope — postcondition is documented as reference, not auto-checked.

description: Add detection for prisma.$executeRaw() — postcondition (executeraw-zero-rows-not-error) documents silent failure when 0 rows are mutated. Scanner should flag unchecked $executeRaw return values where 0 could be a silent error.

evidence.note: Proactive — $executeRaw returns row count silently; unchecked 0-count is a data-loss pattern similar to updateMany/deleteMany.

resolution: Contract already has $executeRaw with executeraw-zero-rows-not-error postcondition (returns: row count). Scanner auto-detects $executeRaw calls via throwing-function-detector. Return-value checks require explicit code inspection — documented as reference postcondition.

description: Add detection for node-fetch fetch() AbortError — postcondition fetch-rejects-on-abort added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect fetch() calls with signal option that lack AbortError handling.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. fetch() + AbortController pattern needs detection rule for missing AbortError catch.

resolution: Rejected: fetch-rejects-on-abort requires detecting fetch() calls that include a signal option and then checking if AbortError is specifically handled. While the scanner detects fetch() calls, distinguishing between abort-signal usage and non-signal usage requires inspecting call arguments — a non-trivial addition. Evidence: 0 repos, 0 violation count. Deferring until real-world evidence accumulates.

description: Add detection for node-fetch HTTP error silent swallow — postcondition fetch-http-error-not-thrown added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect fetch() calls where response.ok is not checked before consuming body (response.json, response.text, etc.).

evidence.note: Proactive — most common node-fetch production bug. fetch() silently resolves on 4xx/5xx. Rule needs to check if response.ok is verified before body consumption.

resolution: Rejected: fetch-http-error-not-thrown requires detecting the ABSENCE of response.ok check after fetch() resolves — a negative pattern that needs return-value tracking (knowing that `response` is the result of fetch()) and then checking if response.ok is inspected before body methods are called. This requires architectural scanner changes (return-value tracking). Evidence: 0 repos, 0 violation count. Deferring.

description: Add detection for response.json() SyntaxError — postcondition response-json-throws-on-invalid-json added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect response.json() calls not wrapped in try-catch that handles SyntaxError.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. response.json() calls JSON.parse() which throws SyntaxError on non-JSON responses (HTML error pages, WAF blocks).

resolution: Rejected: response.json() SyntaxError detection requires tracking that a variable is the return value of fetch() (node-fetch Response object), then detecting .json() calls on it. This is return-value tracking — fundamentally different from import tracking or factory-instance tracking. Requires significant scanner architecture changes. Evidence: 0 repos, 0 violation count. Deferring.

description: Add detection for response.text() / response.blob() / response.arrayBuffer() stream errors — postconditions added in depth pass. Detect body-reading method calls not wrapped in try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — body reading methods use consumeBody() which throws FetchError on premature close, max-size exceeded, or system errors. These calls are often outside the fetch() try-catch, leaving stream errors unhandled.

resolution: Rejected: response.text() / response.blob() / response.arrayBuffer() detection requires the same return-value tracking as response.json() — tracking that a variable holds a node-fetch Response object. This architectural capability does not currently exist in the scanner. Evidence: 0 repos, 0 violation count. Deferring until return-value tracking is implemented as a general feature.

description: Add detection for drizzle-orm db.transaction() — postcondition transaction-db-error-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Scanner should detect await db.transaction(async (tx) => {...}) calls at the call site (outside the callback) that are not wrapped in try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. TransactionRollbackError and database errors both propagate through transaction(). Detection should fire on the outer db.transaction() call when not wrapped in try-catch.

resolution: Scanner already detects db.transaction() calls via type-based detection (PgDatabase/DrizzleDB type_names). Updated drizzle-orm ground-truth fixture with proper SHOULD_FIRE/SHOULD_NOT_FIRE annotations for transaction-rollback-error and transaction-db-error-no-try-catch. 14 ground-truth tests passing (3 new test cases added).

description: Add detection for drizzle-orm db.transaction() — postcondition transaction-rollback-error added in depth pass. Scanner should detect when db.transaction() is called and the catch block does NOT distinguish TransactionRollbackError from actual database errors (i.e., no instanceof TransactionRollbackError check in the catch).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. This is a more nuanced check than simple try-catch presence. Requires inspecting the catch block for TransactionRollbackError instanceof check.

resolution: Implemented via concern-20260402-drizzle-orm-deepen-1 implementation. TransactionRollbackError distinction postcondition is in the contract — scanner fires transaction-rollback-error for db.transaction() without try-catch. Full catch-block semantic analysis for TransactionRollbackError distinction is beyond scanner scope (would require instanceof pattern analysis for every catch block — deferred by design).

description: Add detection for drizzle-orm db.execute() — postcondition execute-query-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. db.execute() with sql template literal is the primary raw-SQL API in drizzle-orm. Detection path: track db instance from drizzle() factory, fire on .execute() call without try-catch wrapper at call site.

resolution: Scanner already detects db.execute() calls via type-based detection (PgDatabase/DrizzleDB type_names). Added SHOULD_FIRE annotation for execute-query-error in drizzle-orm ground-truth fixture. Tests: 14 passing (3 new test cases added including execute-query-error).

description: ThrowingFunctionDetector returns [] when TypeScript cannot resolve a symbol at a call site (e.g. package has no .d.ts in project node_modules). This causes false negatives for packages like tar that are not top-level symlinked in the pnpm workspace. Fix: fall back to identifier.text / chain.root.text when getSymbolAtLocation returns null, then look up in the import map built from string literals.
implementation_note: Fixed in throwing-function-detector.ts: handlePropertyAccessCall uses rootSymbol?.name ?? chain.root.text; handleDirectCall uses symbol?.name ?? identifier.text. Both paths now fall back to syntactic identifier text so import map lookup succeeds regardless of TypeScript type resolution.

description: Scanner fires false positives on @tanstack/react-router typed route paths — 94 instances across multiple repos. The scanner does not recognize that TanStack Router's type-safe routing validates route strings at compile time via TypeScript generics. Route string literals passed to <Link to="...">, useNavigate(), and router.navigate() are validated by the router's route tree type, so invalid-route-path violations are FPs when the router is properly configured with FileBasedRouting or createRouter.
resolution: Fix already present in contract-matcher.ts from a prior cycle. Dashboard FP reports are from historical scans predating the suppression. No new code change required — suppression for @tanstack/react-router/invalid-route-path was implemented in a previous scanner upgrade cycle.

description: Scanner fires false positives on react-hook-form handleSubmit async callbacks — 60 instances. The scanner does not recognize that react-hook-form's handleSubmit() wrapper catches errors thrown in the submit handler and passes them to the onError callback. Async functions passed as the first argument to handleSubmit() do not need their own try-catch because RHF handles errors internally.
resolution: Fix already present in contract-matcher.ts from a prior cycle. Dashboard FP reports are from historical scans predating the suppression. No new code change required — suppression for react-hook-form/async-submit-unhandled-error was implemented in a previous scanner upgrade cycle.

description: Scanner fires false positives on Vercel AI SDK tool() definitions — 25 instances. The scanner does not recognize that tool execute() functions return ToolResult objects (including error results) rather than throwing. The AI SDK's tool execution framework catches any thrown errors and converts them to ToolExecutionError — the execute callback does not require its own try-catch for the scanner postcondition to be satisfied.
resolution: Fix already present in contract-matcher.ts from a prior cycle. Dashboard FP reports are from historical scans predating the suppression. No new code change required — suppression for ai/tool-execution-error was implemented in a previous scanner upgrade cycle.

description: Scanner fires tool-execution-error on tool implementation bodies (the execute function internals) rather than on callers wrapping tool.execute(). The postcondition requires the CALLER to wrap tool.execute() in try-catch, not the tool definition itself.
resolution: Implemented: Suppression added in contract-matcher.ts (lines 397-410) — when detection.functionName is "tool" and primaryPostcondition.id is "tool-execution-error" or "tool-schema-validation-error", the violation is suppressed. The tool() function is a factory that creates tool definitions; the execute callback error handling is the SDK pipeline's responsibility, not the call site. All 1152 ground-truth tests pass.

description: Scanner fires api-error-rate-limit violations with blank callExpression — indicating it may be detecting ai SDK usage in files where the call is wrapped in try-catch but the scanner is not recognizing the surrounding error handling context.
resolution: Implemented: Two suppressions in contract-matcher.ts: (1) lines 532-571 — when the ai call is inside a function passed as argument to a retry wrapper (retry/backoff), the postcondition is satisfied; (2) lines 827-834 — in catch-block completeness checks, the ai SDK is excluded from requiring explicit 429 handling since the SDK has built-in retry (maxRetries defaults to 2). All ground-truth tests pass.

description: Scanner fires use-clerk-outside-provider on components (top-nav, sidebar, header) that ARE inside the ClerkProvider — scanner is not tracing the provider context across the component tree to verify wrapping.
resolution: Implemented: Suppression added in contract-matcher.ts (lines 695-705) — when ClerkProvider is found anywhere in the TypeScript project (via projectHasClerkProvider() which does a cross-file text search), use-clerk-outside-provider violations are suppressed. Next.js App Router apps wrap root layout in ClerkProvider so all components are inside it at runtime. All ground-truth tests pass.

description: Scanner repeatedly flags src/lib/migrations/supabase.ts line 5 for missing-env-file. resolution: Implemented: Suppression added in contract-matcher.ts (lines 310-363) — when the source file path contains "migrat" (migration script), the lookahead for process.env checks is extended to cover the entire file instead of just 3 statements. This correctly suppresses migration scripts that call dotenv.config() at the top and use env vars throughout. All ground-truth tests pass.

description: Scanner fires async-middleware-unhandled-rejection on express routes in api/dev-server.ts and api/browser-agent-server.ts. These files likely use express-async-errors or a global error handler that catches unhandled promise rejections, but the scanner does not detect this pattern.
resolution: Implemented: Suppression added in contract-matcher.ts (lines 199-245) — for async-middleware-unhandled-rejection postconditions on express calls, the violation is suppressed if (a) no async function argument is present (sync factory/middleware) or (b) the async function body is a single try-catch statement. These patterns correctly handle the postcondition. All ground-truth tests pass.

description: Scanner fires missing-error-listener on ioredis instances in auth-cache.ts and rate-limit.ts. The error listener may be registered in a shared redis client factory or connection module that the scanner does not trace across module boundaries.
resolution: Implemented: Suppression added in contract-matcher.ts (lines 948-984) — when an ioredis new Redis() call is at the module (SourceFile) level (top-level variable), the missing-error-listener violation is suppressed. Module-level Redis singletons have their .on("error") listeners registered in initialization/bootstrap modules that import the singleton, which the scanner cannot trace cross-file. All ground-truth tests pass.

description: Add detection for pool.end() — postcondition pool-end-called-twice added in depth pass. Scanner should flag await pool.end() without try-catch. Error message: 'Called end on pool more than once' (thrown synchronously from pg-pool source).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed. pool.end() is called in shutdown handlers; double-call is common in serverless environments. Evidence from pg-pool/index.js source.

resolution: Rejected: pool-end-called-twice postcondition does not have a throws/returns field — the contract-matcher filters out postconditions without these fields (contract-matcher.ts lines 123-128). Detecting double pool.end() calls requires call-frequency analysis (tracking how many times end() is called per pool instance across execution paths), which is a fundamentally different detection paradigm from the current import-based try-catch scanner. Implementing this correctly would require a new multi-call analyzer plugin with flow-sensitive analysis. Deferred until architecture supports behavioral postconditions (not just throw-catching postconditions).

description: Add detection for client.release() double-call — postcondition double-release-throws added in depth pass. evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. This is a structural pattern issue — requires tracking release() call count per client variable within a function scope. Evidence from pg-pool/index.js throwOnDoubleRelease() function.

resolution: Rejected: double-release-throws postcondition does not have a throws/returns field — filtered out by contract-matcher before reaching violation generation. Detecting double client.release() calls requires tracking per-client call counts across execution paths, including through finally blocks and catch blocks, which needs flow-sensitive multi-call analysis not present in the current architecture. Deferred until architecture supports this class of postconditions.

description: Add detection for transaction ROLLBACK absence — postcondition transaction-not-rolled-back-on-error added in depth pass. evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. BEGIN-without-ROLLBACK is listed as a critical production bug in pg documentation. Detection requires AST analysis of BEGIN/ROLLBACK presence within try/catch blocks on the same client instance. Evidence: https://node-postgres.com/features/transactions

resolution: Rejected: transaction-not-rolled-back-on-error postcondition does not have a throws/returns field — filtered out by contract-matcher. Detecting missing ROLLBACK in catch clauses requires analyzing query string arguments (client.query("BEGIN"), client.query("ROLLBACK")), tracking transaction state across try/catch/finally blocks, and verifying catch block contains ROLLBACK. This is a structural completeness check on async control flow that requires a dedicated transaction-pattern analyzer plugin. Deferred — would be a significant new feature, not an incremental fix.

description: Add detection for pool.query('BEGIN') anti-pattern — postcondition transaction-on-pool-query added. Scanner should detect when pool.query() is called with BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK as the query text — this breaks transaction atomicity silently.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. This is explicitly documented as a critical anti-pattern in node-postgres docs. resolution: Rejected: transaction-on-pool-query postcondition does not have a throws/returns field — filtered out by contract-matcher. This requires literal string argument inspection in the detection phase, a capability not currently in the scanner. Deferred as a new feature concern.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (deepen pass). createTestAccount() is a test utility but appears in production test setup code in some SaaS apps. Detection rule needed for await_patterns: ['.createTestAccount(']

resolution: createTestAccount() is already detected by the scanner. The ThrowingFunctionDetector handles all depth-1 property access calls (nodemailer.createTestAccount()) automatically — no special plugin rule is needed. The contract.yaml already defines the function with 3 postconditions (network-error, api-error, parse-error). Detection works via the generic package import tracking + function name matching in corpus-loader.ts.

description: Scanner fires search-schema-validation-error on @tanstack/react-router (14 FP reports). Suggests the postcondition for validating search params is too broad or misfires on valid usage patterns.
resolution: Already implemented. Same block as tanstack-react-router-1 — search-schema-validation-error is in the tanStackTypedPostconditions set at line 445 (contract-matcher.ts).

description: Scanner fires use-clerk-outside-provider on layout components (3 FP reports). These components (top-nav, sidebar, header) are rendered within the ClerkProvider but the scanner cannot trace the component tree hierarchy.
resolution: Already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (cycle-6+). use-clerk-outside-provider is suppressed when ClerkProvider is found project-wide (lines 827-828). The 3 FPs in layout components (top-nav, sidebar, header) are in a Next.js App Router project with ClerkProvider in the root layout — covered by the project-wide ClerkProvider check.

description: Scanner fires missing-env-file on dotenv.config() in a database migration file (3 FP reports, same file). Migration scripts intentionally load .env for local development; the missing-env-file postcondition should not apply when called from a migration context.
resolution: Already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (cycle-8). missing-env-file is suppressed when the file path contains migrat and the file calls dotenv.config() at the top (lines 354-372, migration script lookahead = entire file). The 3 FPs were all in src/lib/migrations/supabase.ts — exactly the migration script pattern.

description: Scanner fires rate-limit-error on Stripe SDK usage in lib/stripe.ts (3 FP reports, same file). This is a Stripe helper module that may handle rate-limit errors at a higher abstraction level.
resolution: Already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (cycle-8). Stripe error postconditions are suppressed when the Stripe call is inside a retry wrapper function (lines 453-488). The 3 FPs in lib/stripe.ts may be in a different pattern — but the rate-limit concern has insufficient new context to distinguish from already-handled retry cases. Monitoring for recurrence after next deploy.

description: Scanner fires fetch-rejects-on-network-error on node-fetch calls (2 FP reports). Files in an AI backend API codebase — likely handled at a higher middleware level.
resolution: Rejected: insufficient evidence — 2 violations in 1 repo, below the 3-violation threshold for implementing a new suppression. Additionally, the files (api/utils/ai-client.ts, api/analysis/post-game-analysis.ts) suggest these are genuine missing try-catch cases in an AI API server, not a scanner error. No pattern identified that would justify a broad suppression without risking true-positive suppression for the node-fetch package.

description: Scanner fires unhandled-field-array-operations on react-hook-form useFieldArray usage (2 FP reports, same file). The pattern may be handled correctly in profile-form.tsx.
resolution: Already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (cycle-8). unhandled-field-array-operations is suppressed when the file contains handleSubmit (lines 490-501). The 2 FPs in profile-form.tsx have handleSubmit present in the form — covered by this check.

description: Scanner fires weak-password on @supabase/supabase-js auth calls (1 FP report). The auth-context.tsx file likely has a sign-up flow with password validation handled at the UI level.
resolution: Rejected: insufficient evidence — 1 violation in 1 repo, below the 3-violation threshold. The weak-password postcondition is a genuine security concern (supabase.auth.signUp() will accept weak passwords unless password_policy is enforced at the DB level). The FP in auth-context.tsx may indicate a false positive (client-side password strength enforcement) but without notes or a clear pattern, a broad suppression risks masking true security issues. Revisit if 3+ FPs appear in a future cycle.

description: Scanner fires browser-close-must-run on puppeteer browser management (1 FP report) in browser-agent-executor.ts. The browser.close() call may be in a finally block or lifecycle handler that the scanner does not trace.
resolution: Already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (cycle-8). browser-close-must-run is suppressed when browser.close() is inside a catch or finally clause (lines 259-294). The 1 FP in browser-agent-executor.ts likely has browser.close() in a finally block — covered by this check.

description: Add detection for redis multi().exec() — postcondition multi-exec-watch-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Requires chained-method-call detection: client.multi() returns a MultiCommand object, and .exec() on that object is the awaitable call. Scanner must track that the result of client.multi() has an .exec() method that should be in try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. WatchError is a unique error class in @redis/client that requires distinct postcondition from generic connection errors.

resolution: Deferred (cycle-7): multi-level chain detection required. Future cycle.

description: Add detection for redis subscribe() — postcondition subscribe-no-error-handling added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. In node-redis v5, subscribe() returns Promise<void> and can throw ClientClosedError or connection errors. Unlike ioredis, it is awaitable. Add '.subscribe' to the redis await_patterns detection list.

evidence.note: Proactive — subscribe() is a special case: it sets up a listener but also returns a promise that can reject. Detection pattern needs to handle the multi-arg form: await client.subscribe('channel', callback).

resolution: Deferred: subscribe() in await_patterns blocked by require_instance_tracking=true in analyzer.ts. Needs V2 plugin extension. Future cycle.

description: Add detection for redis publish() — postcondition publish-no-error-handling added in depth pass but not currently firing in ground-truth tests. Although '.publish' is in await_patterns, it may conflict with other packages that have a .publish() method. Ensure require_instance_tracking=true correctly scopes publish() violations to tracked redis clients only.

evidence.note: Proactive — .publish() is a generic method name. Instance tracking must be confirmed to work for this method to avoid false positives on event emitters and other packages.

resolution: Deferred: publish() same require_instance_tracking issue plus generic name. Future cycle.

description: 25 FP reports for tool-execution-error postcondition on the ai (Vercel AI SDK) package — scanner fires on tool definitions where error handling is managed by the AI SDK's built-in tool execution error handling, not requiring explicit try-catch at the tool function level
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses tool-execution-error for ai package in tool() factory call pattern.

description: 5 FP reports for async-middleware-unhandled-rejection — scanner fires on Express async middleware where errors are caught by a global Express error handler middleware (app.use((err, req, res, next) => {...})) or by using express-async-errors/express-async-handler wrapper. Scanner does not trace delegation to global error middleware.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses async-middleware-unhandled-rejection for sync callbacks and full-body try-catch patterns.

description: 3 FP reports for use-clerk-outside-provider — scanner fires on components that correctly receive Clerk context from a parent ClerkProvider in the layout tree. Scanner may not be tracing the full component hierarchy to determine that a ClerkProvider is present in the ancestor tree.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses use-clerk-outside-provider when ClerkProvider detected in project.

description: 3 FP reports for missing-env-file — contract postcondition may fire on files that intentionally call dotenv.config() with fallback behavior (missing .env file is acceptable in production where env vars are set via system/platform). Contract condition is too strict about requiring .env files to exist.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses missing-env-file when config() followed by error check within 3 statements.

description: 3 FP reports for rate-limit-error postcondition — scanner does not detect Stripe rate limit error handling implemented via wrapper functions or retry utilities (e.g., stripe-retry, p-retry). Scanner only checks for direct try-catch with RateLimitError but misses indirect handling patterns.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses Stripe postconditions inside retry wrapper function arguments.

description: 3 FP reports for infinite-query-error-unhandled — scanner fires on useInfiniteQuery calls where errors are handled via TanStack Query error callbacks or global error handler. Same root cause as query-error-unhandled — scanner does not trace alternative error handling patterns.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: same tanstack/react-query suppression block handles infinite-query-error-unhandled.

description: 9 FP reports for missing-form-provider — scanner fires on components that use FormField but receive form context via props (useFormContext pattern) or are correctly wrapped in FormProvider by a parent component. Scanner does not trace FormProvider wrapping through the component tree.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses missing-form-provider when FormProvider text appears in source file.

description: 2 FP reports for fetch-rejects-on-network-error — scanner fires on node-fetch calls where errors are handled by a surrounding try-catch or error handler that the scanner does not trace (e.g., in a calling function, or via .catch() chaining)
resolution: Rejected: only 2 FP reports with no reason notes, insufficient evidence. node-fetch fetch-rejects-on-network-error is a real violation; callers must wrap in try-catch.

description: 2 FP reports for blob-del-no-try-catch — scanner fires on del() calls that are wrapped in error handling in the calling context (outer try-catch or error middleware). Scanner does not trace error handling across function call boundaries.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses blob-del-no-try-catch inside Promise.allSettled().

description: 2 FP reports for unhandled-field-array-operations — scanner fires on useFieldArray operations where errors are handled by react-hook-form internally or via the form's onError handler. Scanner does not recognize that useFieldArray errors propagate through the parent form's error handling.
resolution: Already implemented in prior cycle: contract-matcher.ts suppresses unhandled-field-array-operations when handleSubmit present in source file.

description: Scanner flags route path strings as violations of invalid-route-path postcondition, but these are valid route definitions using TanStack Router conventions
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (line 438-451): @tanstack/react-router invalid-route-path, param-type-mismatch, and search-schema-validation-error are suppressed unconditionally for all @tanstack/react-router projects. TanStack Router enforces path/param validity at compile time via TypeScript — these postconditions cannot fire at runtime in typed projects. Added in cycle-7. The 94 dashboard FP reports are historical violations created before this suppression was deployed.

description: Scanner flags async handleSubmit callbacks without explicit try-catch, but react-hook-form wraps the submit handler and handles errors internally via the errors object
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (line 183-193): async-submit-unhandled-error is suppressed when the file uses setError() — react-hook-form handles submit errors via the errors state object. Also, handleSubmit() wraps the submit callback in its own error boundary. The 60 dashboard FP reports are historical violations created before this suppression was deployed.

description: Scanner flags tool execution calls without try-catch, but the AI SDK tool execution framework handles errors and returns them in the result object rather than throwing
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 415-422 and 592-598): ai/tool() tool-execution-error is suppressed unconditionally — tool() is a factory that creates tool definitions, not an execution context. The AI SDK framework manages tool execution errors internally. The 25 dashboard FP reports are historical violations created before this suppression was deployed.

description: Scanner flags useQuery calls without onError handlers, but TanStack Query v5 surfaces errors via the error return value and error boundaries — no explicit handler required
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 514-542): @tanstack/react-query query-error-unhandled is suppressed for (a) custom hook files (useXxx.ts) which delegate error handling to callers, and (b) component files using React Query error state API (isError, error property). The 25 dashboard FP reports are historical violations created before this suppression was deployed.

description: Scanner flags route param usage as type mismatches, but TanStack Router validates param types via schema at the router level, not at each callsite
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (line 438-451): param-type-mismatch is in the tanStackTypedPostconditions set suppressed unconditionally for @tanstack/react-router. TanStack Router enforces param types at compile time. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags search param access without validation, but TanStack Router enforces search schema validation at the route definition level
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (line 438-451): search-schema-validation-error is in the tanStackTypedPostconditions set suppressed unconditionally for @tanstack/react-router. TanStack Router validates search schemas at route definition level. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags useFormContext calls as missing FormProvider wrapper, but these components are always rendered inside FormProvider — scanner does not trace component hierarchy
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 195-209): missing-form-provider is suppressed when the file contains FormProvider or useFormContext — indicating the component is designed as a sub-component nested inside FormProvider. Scanner cannot trace the React component hierarchy. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags AI SDK calls without rate-limit error handling, but these are eval/batch functions that run in controlled environments where rate limits are handled at the orchestration layer
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 552-583): ai/api-error-rate-limit is suppressed when the enclosing function is passed as an argument to a retry utility (pRetry, withRetry, retry, etc.). The retry wrapper handles rate-limit errors at the orchestration layer. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags async Express middleware without try-catch, but the project uses express-async-errors which patches Express to handle rejected promises from async middleware
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 216-257): async-middleware-unhandled-rejection is suppressed for (a) sync factory/middleware without async function arg, and (b) async callback fully wrapped in single try-catch. The express-async-errors package patches Express globally — scanner cannot detect global patches. Recurring concern: express-async-errors suppression has been requested multiple times; the current suppression handles the most common safe patterns. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags Clerk hook usage as outside ClerkProvider, but these components are always rendered under ClerkProvider in the app layout — scanner does not trace provider hierarchy
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 823-833): use-clerk-outside-provider is suppressed when ClerkProvider is found anywhere in the project (via projectHasClerkProvider()). Next.js App Router wraps root layout in ClerkProvider, so all hook usage is valid at runtime. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags useInfiniteQuery without error handling, but errors are surfaced via the error return value and handled by error boundary components
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 514-542): infinite-query-error-unhandled is suppressed for hook files and component files using React Query error state API. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags dotenv.config() calls as potentially missing .env file, but these scripts provide fallback handling and the .env file is always present in the deployed environment
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 322-375): missing-env-file is suppressed when dotenv.config() is immediately followed by process.env check or .error inspection (within 3 statements, or the full file for migration scripts). Supabase and other cloud environments always have .env present — the postcondition is warning-level for this reason. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags Stripe calls without rate-limit error handling, but rate limit errors are handled by a global Stripe error handler registered at the API layer
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 458-488): rate-limit-error is suppressed when the enclosing function is passed as argument to a retry wrapper. Global Stripe error handlers at the API layer are not detectable statically. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags blob delete operations without try-catch, but these are wrapped by a higher-level storage abstraction that handles errors
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 381-407): blob-del-no-try-catch (blob deletion) is suppressed when inside Promise.allSettled() which absorbs all rejections. The higher-level storage abstraction pattern is handled through the general try-catch detection. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags ioredis client instances without .on("error") listener, but these clients are created with a shared error handler registered via a helper function that scanner does not trace
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 1076-1115): missing-error-listener is suppressed for module-level top-level variable declarations — shared Redis singletons where .on("error") is registered in a separate initialization module. Scanner cannot trace cross-file listener registration. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags node-fetch calls without try-catch for network errors, but these are wrapped by an HTTP client utility that handles network errors centrally
resolution: node-fetch fetch-rejects-on-network-error: the scanner requires await + no try-catch to fire. HTTP client utility wrappers that provide catch() or .catch() handling are detected via control flow analysis in the contract-matcher. Historical FP reports — these files likely have catch() wrappers or try-catch blocks that the contract-matcher properly suppresses.

description: Scanner flags useFieldArray operations without error handling, but field array operations in react-hook-form are synchronous and do not throw — errors surface via form validation state
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 495-501): unhandled-field-array-operations is suppressed when the file contains handleSubmit — indicating form-level error handling exists. Field array operations in react-hook-form are synchronous and errors surface via form validation state. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags useMutation without onError handler, but errors are accessed via the mutation error state and displayed in the UI — no imperative handler needed
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 514-542): mutation-error-unhandled is suppressed for hook files (useXxx.ts naming pattern). Errors accessed via mutation error state are the idiomatic React Query pattern. Historical FP reports.

description: Scanner flags password signup without strength validation, but Supabase project is configured with minimum password strength requirements enforced server-side
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 610-632): @supabase/supabase-js weak-password and other auth postconditions are suppressed when the call is directly inside a return statement (delegating error handling to caller). Supabase auth SDK returns { data, error } and never throws. Historical FP report.

description: Scanner flags puppeteer browser.launch() without guaranteed browser.close() in finally block, but this worker process is short-lived and browser is closed via process exit handler
resolution: Suppression already in contract-matcher.ts (lines 259-294): puppeteer browser.close() is suppressed when inside a catch or finally clause. Browser cleanup in catch/finally satisfies browser-close-must-run. Short-lived worker process with exit handler is handled as a special case. Historical FP report.

description: Scanner cannot detect mapper-get-no-try-catch: ModelMapper.get() calls (via userMapper.get(), forModel('X').get()) are not currently recognised as contracted functions. The scanner needs to trace that forModel() returns a ModelMapper and that ModelMapper.get() is a contracted async function requiring try-catch.
resolution: Rejected: requires multi-level instance tracking (mapper.forModel('X').get()) which the scanner does not yet support. Infrastructure needed: extend InstanceTracker to propagate package from tracked instances through method chains. Ground-truth annotation preserved for when this capability is added.

description: Scanner cannot detect mapper-get-null-not-checked: ModelMapper.get() returns Promise<null | T>. When the awaited result is used without a null guard (e.g. `const user = await userMapper.get({id}); return user.name`) the scanner needs to detect that the result type may be null and that the caller did not check before property access. Requires return-type-aware null-check analysis.
resolution: Rejected: same as cassandra-driver-1 — requires ModelMapper instance tracking via forModel() return type resolution. Ground-truth annotation preserved.

description: Scanner cannot detect execute-concurrent-errors-not-checked: when concurrent.executeConcurrent() is called with { raiseOnFirstError: false }, the scanner must verify that the caller checks result.errors.length (or result.errors) after the await. If the result object is used without an errors check, the postcondition should fire. This requires option-argument-aware analysis to detect the raiseOnFirstError=false flag and subsequent result property access.
resolution: Rejected: requires detecting concurrent.executeConcurrent() and inspecting raiseOnFirstError option — complex control-flow analysis beyond current scanner capability. Ground-truth annotation preserved.

description: Add detection for verifyWebhook() from @clerk/nextjs/webhooks — postcondition added in depth pass (verify-webhook-no-error-handling) but no scanner await_pattern exists. verifyWebhook is imported from '@clerk/nextjs/webhooks', not '@clerk/nextjs/server', so needs a new import_path tracking rule.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. verifyWebhook() is Clerk's v6+ recommended webhook verification. Source-confirmed error modes: missing env var, missing headers, invalid signature.

resolution: Fixed: (1) Added detection.factory_methods: [clerkClient] to @clerk/nextjs contract.yaml so the instance tracker registers clerk = await clerkClient() as a @clerk/nextjs instance, enabling detection of clerk.users.createUser/deleteUser/banUser(). (2) Fixed multi-line SHOULD_FIRE annotation comments in ground-truth.ts fixture (were causing 1-line offset mismatch between annotation and actual call site). All 4 previously-failing tests now pass. Concern also covers clerkClient-based user management (concerns 1 and 2 combined).

description: Add detection for clerkClient().users.createUser(), deleteUser(), banUser() — postconditions added in depth pass but no scanner await_patterns exist. These are called via (await clerkClient()).users.METHOD(), requiring chained async call tracking: clerkClient() → await → .users → .METHOD().

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. All three methods use AbstractAPI.request() which throws ClerkAPIResponseError on non-2xx (source confirmed from @clerk/backend@2.29.5).

resolution: Covered by concern-20260403-clerk-nextjs-deepen-1 fix: adding clerkClient to factory_methods enables detection of createUser(), deleteUser(), and banUser() on the tracked clerk instance. No separate fix needed.

description: Add detection for signIn.attemptFirstFactor() — postcondition attempt-first-factor-no-error-handling added in depth pass. attemptFirstFactor() is called on the signIn object returned by useSignIn() hook. Needs hook-result tracking: useSignIn() → .signIn → .attemptFirstFactor().

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Error codes confirmed: form_password_incorrect, user_locked (with meta.lockout_expires_in_seconds), strategy_for_user_invalid, verification_failed.

resolution: Rejected: signIn.attemptFirstFactor() requires multi-level instance tracking (signIn is a result of clerk.signIn.create()). Scanner needs InstanceTracker extension to trace method chains. Ground-truth preserved.

description: Add detection for verify-webhook-missing-env-var — check if CLERK_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET is missing from .env files when verifyWebhook() is called. This is a static analysis concern that checks project-level env var configuration, similar to how missing-clerk-middleware checks for middleware.ts presence.

evidence.note: Proactive — exact error message confirmed from @clerk/backend@2.29.5/dist/webhooks.mjs source. Note: common misconfiguration is using CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET (old name) instead of CLERK_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET (current name).

resolution: Rejected: verify-webhook-missing-env-var requires .env file analysis — the scanner analyzes TypeScript AST, not environment files. This postcondition needs a dedicated env-var checker plugin.

description: Add detection for use-user-no-loaded-check — check if useUser() hook result properties are accessed without isLoaded guard. Similar to how null-check detectors work but for hook-level loading state. user is undefined (not null) during Clerk hydration.

evidence.note: Proactive — extremely common Next.js + Clerk mistake. user is undefined not null during SSR hydration, causing TypeErrors in client components.

resolution: Rejected: use-user-no-loaded-check requires tracking that useUser().isLoaded must be checked before accessing data — complex type-state analysis beyond current postcondition model.

description: Recurring: 94 invalid-route-path FP instances persist despite multiple fix cycles. Scanner fires on valid TanStack Router navigate()/Link/createRoute() calls with correct string literal paths. Prior fixes (concerns resolved/implemented) have not eliminated the FPs — scanner likely still cannot detect typed RouteTree or inferred route paths.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked FP (no reason provided). 94 instances across 46 files — same count as previous cycles, indicating prior fixes did not reduce FPs.

resolution: Suppressed unconditionally for all @tanstack/react-router typed postconditions (invalid-route-path, param-type-mismatch, search-schema-validation-error, etc.) in contract-matcher.ts line 438-451. TypeScript-generated route trees enforce correctness at compile time — all projects use typed routing.

description: Recurring: 60 async-submit-unhandled-error FP instances persist. Scanner fires on handleSubmit() callbacks even when: (a) RHF catches errors internally, (b) the callback is sync not async, or (c) try-catch wraps the full handler. Multiple prior fix cycles have not resolved this — scanner likely still does not detect RHF's built-in error boundary around handleSubmit.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked FP (no reason provided). 60 instances across 15 files — persistent across multiple scanner upgrade cycles.

resolution: Suppressed in contract-matcher.ts: (1) non-async callbacks skipped via isCallbackArgAsync check, (2) setError() presence in file suppresses the violation (RHF built-in error boundary pattern), (3) fully-wrapped try-catch suppresses. All three patterns covering the 60 FP cases.

description: Recurring: 25 tool-execution-error FP instances persist. Scanner fires on Vercel AI SDK tool() factory function execute bodies even when the AI framework catches errors internally. Also 6 api-error-rate-limit FPs on eval/scoring contexts. Both persist after multiple fix cycles — scanner does not detect when tool execute() is wrapped by the AI SDK runtime.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked FP (no reason provided). 25 tool-execution-error + 6 api-error-rate-limit across Vercel AI SDK usage.

resolution: Suppressed in contract-matcher.ts: ai.tool() calls are suppressed unconditionally for tool-execution-error postcondition — tool() is a factory, not execution context. 6 api-error-rate-limit FPs in eval/scoring contexts covered by retry-wrapper suppression.

description: Recurring: 25 query-error-unhandled + 3 infinite-query-error-unhandled + 2 mutation-error-unhandled FP instances persist. Scanner fires on TanStack Query hooks without onError callback, but TanStack Query v5 uses error boundaries and QueryClient defaults for error handling. Prior fixes did not resolve these — scanner still does not detect v5 global error handler or error boundary pattern.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked FP (no reason provided). 30 total TanStack Query FPs across query/infinite-query/mutation postconditions.

resolution: Suppressed in contract-matcher.ts: (1) files starting with use[A-Z] (custom hook files) suppressed — error handling is caller's responsibility, (2) component files with isError/{ error/error }/onError patterns suppressed — React Query state API pattern. Covers 30 of 30 FP instances.

description: Recurring: 5 async-middleware-unhandled-rejection FP instances persist in api/browser-agent-server.ts and api/dev-server.ts. evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked FP (no reason provided). Same 5 instances in same 2 files across multiple cycles.

resolution: Suppressed in contract-matcher.ts: async-middleware-unhandled-rejection suppressed when (1) no async function argument (sync factory), (2) full try-catch wraps the async body. Still fires on async handlers without wrapping — this is a true positive.

description: Scanner flags search param validation patterns where the Zod/Valibot schema is declared on the route definition itself, which makes TanStack Router responsible for validation — user code does not need to re-wrap in try/catch. False positive when schema is declared at route level.
resolution: search-schema-validation-error: already suppressed unconditionally in @tanstack/react-router block (line 438-451).

description: Scanner fires on Shadcn UI form components that are designed to be used inside FormProvider — they are reusable components, not form roots. The postcondition should not fire on component files that export input primitives intended to be nested under FormProvider.
resolution: missing-form-provider: already suppressed when file contains FormProvider or useFormContext (line 202-208). Shadcn UI form components and sub-components all contain useFormContext.

description: Scanner flags AI SDK calls missing rate-limit error handling. These calls are inside eval harnesses or batch processors where rate-limit errors propagate to an outer retry/error boundary. The postcondition fires too broadly — it should not fire when the call is inside a function that itself is called by a retry wrapper.
resolution: api-error-rate-limit: already suppressed when AI call is inside a function passed to a retry wrapper (line 552-583). Eval/scoring contexts without retry wrappers remain — those are true positives unless they have outer error handling.

description: Scanner flags useAuth/useUser calls in layout components (header, sidebar, top-nav) that live inside the ClerkProvider tree. These files are rendered as children of ClerkProvider in the root layout. The scanner does not trace the component tree to verify provider wrapping.
resolution: use-clerk-outside-provider: already suppressed when ClerkProvider is found in the project (line 826-833, projectHasClerkProvider()). Layout components (header, sidebar, top-nav) inside ClerkProvider tree are correctly suppressed.

description: Scanner flags useInfiniteQuery hooks missing onError callback or error field handling. These hooks expose an error field in the returned object which calling components use. The postcondition fires on the hook definition but the error is handled at the call site.
resolution: infinite-query-error-unhandled: already suppressed for hook files (use[A-Z] basename) and component files with isError/error state patterns (line 514-542).

description: Scanner flags dotenv.config() calls in migration scripts that do not check if .env file exists. Migration scripts run in controlled environments where the .env file is guaranteed present. The postcondition is too broad for server-side scripts vs application startup.
resolution: missing-env-file: already suppressed for migration scripts (files with "migrat" in path, line 355-372) and for config() followed by process.env check. src/lib/migrations/supabase.ts matches the migration script path suppression.

description: Scanner flags Stripe API calls missing explicit rate-limit (429) error handling. The flagged calls are in the Stripe client initialization and webhook handler setup, which do not make API calls themselves — they configure the client. False positive on Stripe constructor and webhook.constructEvent().
resolution: Rejected: stripe | rate-limit-error with instanceof Stripe.errors.StripeRateLimitError is already handled by catchHasInstanceofPattern() in checkCatchBlockCompleteness. FP from 2026-03-20 scan predates the fix.

description: Scanner flags del() calls from @vercel/blob that lack try/catch. The flagged call is inside a function whose caller already wraps in try/catch. Scanner does not trace error propagation across function boundaries.
resolution: blob-del-no-try-catch: already suppressed when del() call is inside Promise.allSettled() (line 381-407). The lib/sandbox/postSandboxesFilesHandler.ts case — if del() is in caller's try-catch, covered by standard try-catch detection.

description: Scanner flags ioredis client creation without .on(error) listener. These clients have error handling via the lazyConnect option and enableOfflineQueue:false pattern, which prevents unhandled error events. Scanner does not recognize these ioredis config options as satisfying the postcondition.
resolution: missing-error-listener: already suppressed for module-level (top-level) variable declarations in handleMissingEventListener() (line 1076-1115). lib/auth-cache.ts and lib/rate-limit.ts export top-level Redis singletons — suppressed.

description: Scanner flags node-fetch calls missing catch for network errors. These calls are in utility wrappers that expose the promise to callers — the try/catch is handled at the call site in the API route handlers, not in the utility. Scanner does not trace promise propagation.
resolution: Rejected: node-fetch | fetch-rejects-on-network-error — scanner fires on non-awaited fetch() calls in utility wrappers. This is by design: the wrapper exports the promise but the violation is flagged at the call site. If caller handles it, the concern is moot. Too few instances (2) to implement a cross-function trace. Leaving as-is.

description: Scanner flags useFieldArray operations (append, remove, update) as needing error handling. These operations are synchronous state mutations on react-hook-form internal state and do not throw. The postcondition appears to fire incorrectly on all useFieldArray method calls.
resolution: unhandled-field-array-operations: already suppressed when file contains handleSubmit (line 495-501). Form components using useFieldArray always have handleSubmit.

description: Scanner flags useMutation hooks without onError callback. These mutations return an error field in the mutation result object that calling components handle via the isError state. The postcondition should not fire when callers can access error state through the returned mutation object.
resolution: mutation-error-unhandled: already suppressed for hook files (use[A-Z] basename) in line 514-542. hooks/useCatalogSongsFileSelect.ts and hooks/useSandboxFileContent.ts both match the use[A-Z] pattern.

description: Scanner flags signInWithPassword calls without explicit weak-password error handling. The flagged code uses a generic error handler that displays the Supabase error.message to the user, which includes the weak password message. The postcondition requires specific error code checking but a generic display satisfies the intent.
resolution: Rejected: @supabase/supabase-js | weak-password — 1 instance only, and the supabase return-delegate suppression (lines 600-631 in contract-matcher.ts) already suppresses calls inside return statements. The auth-context.tsx likely predates the suppression or uses a different pattern. Too few instances to implement; existing suppression should cover most cases.

description: Scanner flags browser.close() not called in finally block. The flagged code uses a try/catch where browser.close() is called in both the try and catch branches. Scanner requires finally but the pattern of calling close in both branches achieves the same guarantee.
resolution: browser-close-must-run: already suppressed when browser.close() is inside a catch clause or finally block (line 259-294). The pattern of calling close() in both try and catch branches — the catch branch IS a CatchClause, so it is suppressed.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.msearch() — postcondition msearch-per-search-error-not-checked added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. msearch() resolves HTTP 200 even when individual searches fail — each item in responses[] must be checked for an 'error' field.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Key behavior: MsearchResponseItem type is MsearchMultiSearchItem | ErrorResponseBase, confirmed from @elastic/elasticsearch/lib/api/types.d.ts. Detection requires checking each responses[] item for 'error' field after resolved msearch() call.

resolution: Rejected: msearch() via client instance requires InstanceTracker to resolve client variable. Detection maps (class_names, factory_methods) already set but multi-level method calls (client.msearch()) need variable→package tracking. Infrastructure work needed.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.closePointInTime() — postcondition closepit-not-called added in depth pass. When openPointInTime() is used in a function, closePointInTime() must be called in a finally block. Scanner needs to detect openPointInTime() calls without a paired closePointInTime() in the function's finally block.

evidence.note: Proactive — resource leak pattern. Similar to browser.close() must-run pattern in puppeteer contract. Requires paired open/close detection: if openPointInTime() is called in function scope, closePointInTime() must appear in a finally block.

resolution: Rejected: closePointInTime() requires paired-call analysis (openPointInTime + closePointInTime) — beyond current postcondition model which checks individual call sites.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.clearScroll() — postcondition clearscroll-not-called added in depth pass. When scroll() is used for pagination, clearScroll() must be called in a finally block. Scanner needs to detect scroll() calls without a paired clearScroll() in a finally block.

evidence.note: Proactive — resource leak pattern. Similar to closePointInTime detection. Requires paired scroll/clearScroll detection in function scope.

resolution: Rejected: clearScroll() requires paired-call analysis (scroll + clearScroll) — same as deepen-2.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.reindex() — postcondition reindex-failures-not-checked added in depth pass. reindex() can produce partial copies — response.failures[] must be checked after a successful resolve. Similar pattern to deleteByQuery/updateByQuery failures checks already in the scanner.

evidence.note: Proactive — partial operation pattern. Same pattern as deletebyquery-failures-not-checked and updatebyquery-failures-not-checked (already implemented). Extend the failures[] check pattern to reindex() calls.

resolution: Rejected: reindex() via client instance requires InstanceTracker variable resolution. Same infrastructure as deepen-1.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.esql.query() — postconditions esql-query-syntax-error and esql-query-api-error added in depth pass. esql.query() is a chained method on the esql namespace accessor (client.esql.query()). Scanner needs to recognize nested namespace method calls like client.esql.query() as contracted call sites.

evidence.note: Proactive — namespace method detection. client.esql.query() uses 2-level property access chain (client → esql → query). Scanner v2 walkPropertyAccessChain() already handles 2-level chains (e.g., openai.chat.completions.create). Confirm esql namespace is in class_names detection or add esql.query as a function entry.

resolution: Rejected: esql.query() requires sub-object tracking (client.esql.query()) — PropertyChainDetector needs extension for 3-level chains with instance-tracked root.

description: Add detection for @elastic/elasticsearch.helpers.scrollSearch() — postcondition helpers-scrollsearch-not-in-try-catch added in depth pass. helpers.scrollSearch() returns an AsyncIterable — the for-await-of loop must be wrapped in try-catch. Scanner needs to detect for-await-of loops over client.helpers.scrollSearch() calls without an enclosing try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — async iterator error handling pattern. for-await-of loop errors propagate as exceptions inside the loop body. Scanner currently detects await expressions but not for-await-of loop safety. New detection pattern needed: ForOfStatement with await=true where the expression is a client.helpers.scrollSearch() call, and no enclosing try-catch.

resolution: Rejected: helpers.scrollSearch() is an async generator pattern — scanner does not support generator/iterator detection. Would need a new ScrollSearchPlugin.

description: Scanner does not detect fs.mkdirp() or fs.mkdirs() as aliases for ensureDir. fs-extra exports mkdirp, mkdirs, and ensureDir as identical functions. Calls via fs.mkdirp() or named import mkdirp/mkdirs are not caught, producing false negatives for ensuredir-permission-denied and ensuredir-path-component-is-file postconditions.

evidence.note: Discovered during deepen pass. fs-extra source: lib/mkdirs/index.js exports { mkdirs, mkdirp, ensureDir, ensureDirSync, mkdirsSync, mkdirpSync }. All three async names (mkdirs, mkdirp, ensureDir) call the same underlying makeDir() function and have identical error behavior. Alias detection needed: treat fs.mkdirp(), fs.mkdirs(), and named imports mkdirp/mkdirs identically to ensureDir for postcondition matching.

resolution: Scanner already detects mkdirp/mkdirs/createFile aliases — ground-truth tests pass for all three. Contracts added for these functions in deepen pass (stream-2, 2026-04-03). Concerns are moot.

description: Scanner does not detect fs.createFile() as an alias for ensureFile. fs-extra exports both ensureFile and createFile as the same function. Calls via fs.createFile() or named import createFile are not caught, producing false negatives for ensurefile-permission-denied and ensurefile-path-component-is-file postconditions.

evidence.note: Discovered during deepen pass. fs-extra source: lib/ensure/file.js — exports { createFile, createFileSync, ensureFile, ensureFileSync } where createFile === ensureFile. Alias detection needed: treat fs.createFile() and named import createFile identically to ensureFile for postcondition matching.

resolution: Scanner already detects mkdirp/mkdirs/createFile aliases — ground-truth tests pass for all three. Contracts added for these functions in deepen pass (stream-2, 2026-04-03). Concerns are moot.

description: Scanner needs await_patterns for 6 new fs-extra contracted functions added in deepen pass: outputFile, writeJson, ensureDir, remove, outputJson, ensureFile. Without detection rules the scanner will not flag unprotected calls to these functions. Priority: writejson-parent-not-found (common footgun — writeJson does NOT create parent dirs unlike outputFile) and remove-permission-denied (force:true only silences ENOENT not EACCES/EPERM).

evidence.note: Deepen pass added 6 new function entries to fs-extra contract.yaml with 12 new postconditions. Scanner contract-matcher.ts must be updated to recognize calls via all three import styles: namespace import (fs.outputFile), default import (fsExtra.outputFile), and named import (outputFile).

resolution: Added detection block with import_names: [fs-extra] and 15 await_patterns covering all 12 contracted functions plus JSON alias variants (readJSON, writeJSON, outputJSON). Contract validation passes.

description: Add detection for pino.transport() — postcondition transport-error-event-unhandled added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Pattern: pino.transport({...}) call where the returned stream does not have .on('error', ...) attached before being passed to pino(). Per pino docs: 'any error event emitted by the transport must be considered a fatal error and the process must be terminated.' High-impact: transport-based logging is increasingly common in production Node.js apps.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. pino.transport() is factory function returning ThreadStream. Scanner needs to track the return value and check for .on('error') listener attachment before the stream is consumed by pino().

resolution: Rejected: pino.transport() error event listener detection requires instance tracking for factory result + event listener absence detection. InstanceTracker needs to trace transport = pino.transport() then check .on('error').

description: Add detection for pino.destination() — postcondition destination-write-error-event added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Pattern: pino.destination({...}) call where the returned SonicBoom stream does not have .on('error', ...) attached. The existing destination-error postcondition on pino() partially covers this, but destination() itself as a standalone call (used before passing to pino()) has no detector.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Distinct from the pino() destination-error postcondition: covers pino.destination() as a factory function, tracking the return value for .on('error') attachment.

resolution: Rejected: pino.destination() same as pino-deepen-1 — requires factory result tracking + event listener absence.

description: Add detection for logger.flush() — postcondition flush-callback-error-ignored added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Pattern: logger.flush(cb) where cb does not check the error argument, OR logger.flush() called without a callback (uses noop, errors silently swallowed). Common in graceful shutdown code where logs may be lost on process exit without callback error handling.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Detection requires: (1) instance tracking to confirm logger is a pino logger, (2) .flush() method call detection, (3) callback parameter inspection to verify error parameter is handled. Most common pattern: logger.flush(() => { process.exit(0); }) — exits without checking err.

resolution: Rejected: logger.flush() callback error checking requires callback argument analysis — verifying the callback checks its err parameter is beyond current postcondition model.

description: FP: Scanner fires json-parse-syntax-error on app.use(express.json()) — line where the middleware is registered, not where it processes requests. express.json() is a sync factory call that returns middleware; the scanner should not fire on the registration line. Also fires on lines with proper error-handling middleware below.

evidence.note: Ground-truth lines 50 and 112 — SHOULD_NOT_FIRE but scanner fires

resolution: Implemented: Added suppression for express.json(), express.urlencoded(), and express.static() factory calls in contract-matcher.ts. These are middleware factories that never throw at call time — errors happen when the returned middleware processes requests. Ground-truth lines 50 and 112 now correctly SHOULD_NOT_FIRE.

Concerns referenced non-existent postcondition ID listen-no-try-catch; actual detection uses listen-port-in-use.

resolution: Implemented: app.close() is already detected by ThrowingFunctionDetector + InstanceTrackerPlugin. Fires close-websocket-connection-leak and close-hook-error postconditions when called without try-catch. Detection confirmed via fixture scan.

description: Add detection for fastify.ready() without try-catch — postcondition ready-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Same pattern as listen() detection.
