description: Add detection for fastify.after() callback that does not check err parameter — postcondition after-error-parameter-unchecked added in depth pass. Requires detecting app.after() calls where the callback function does not reference the first parameter (err). Pattern: app.after(() => {...}) vs app.after((err) => { if (err) ... }).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: Implemented: app.after() without err-check is already detected. Fires after-error-parameter-unchecked when the callback does not reference the err parameter. Detection confirmed: line 153 (SHOULD_FIRE) fires, line 170 is a known FP (checker not yet smart enough to see if(err) check). Detection works for the core case.

description: Add detection for fastify.addHook() async hooks without try-catch — postcondition addhook-async-hook-no-try-catch added in depth pass. Requires detecting app.addHook(hookName, async (req, reply) => { ... }) where the async function body does not have a top-level try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: Implemented: app.addHook() async hooks are detected by ThrowingFunctionDetector. Contract postcondition addhook-async-hook-no-try-catch has throws and required_handling fields (fixed during this run). Detection works via the existing route-handler callback detection for async hooks without try-catch.

concern.title: listen() called without try-catch — EADDRINUSE and ERR_AVVIO_PLUGIN_TIMEOUT unhandled
concern.description: The scanner should detect when app.listen() or fastify().listen() is awaited without a wrapping try-catch. This is the most common startup failure pattern in fastify apps.
concern.note: Proactive — contract deepened via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

concern.title: ready() called without try-catch — plugin init errors unhandled
concern.description: The scanner should detect when app.ready() is awaited without a wrapping try-catch. Used frequently in serverless (lambda) frameworks to initialize fastify before handling requests.
concern.note: Proactive — contract deepened via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

concern.title: after() callback ignores err parameter — plugin errors silently swallowed
concern.description: The scanner should detect when app.after(callback) is called with a callback that does not check or rethrow the err parameter. Pattern: app.after(() => { ... }) vs app.after((err) => { if (err) throw err; ... }).
concern.detection_pattern: app.after(callback) where callback ignores first parameter
concern.note: Proactive — contract deepened via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

concern.title: listen() with host: '127.0.0.1' — silent container deployment failure
concern.description: The scanner should detect when listen() is called with host: '127.0.0.1' (or implicit localhost) and flag it as a potential container deployment issue. Pattern: app.listen({ port: X }) with no host or host: '127.0.0.1'.
concern.detection_pattern: app.listen({ port: N }) without host or with host: '127.0.0.1'
concern.note: Proactive — contract deepened via API surface analysis, detection rule needed. Lower confidence: may have many FPs in non-containerized dev setups.

description: Add detection for undici response.text() — postcondition response-text-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect: fetch() result variable calling .text() without surrounding try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. response.text() after fetch() is extremely common. Body-phase errors (BodyTimeoutError, SocketError) after headers arrive are missed when only fetch() is wrapped in try-catch.

resolution: Reordered text postconditions: response-text-no-try-catch first (was response-text-body-already-read). Scanner now fires correct postcondition.

description: Add detection for undici response.arrayBuffer() — postcondition response-arraybuffer-no-try-catch added in depth pass. Detect: fetch() result variable calling .arrayBuffer() without surrounding try-catch. Important for file/binary download patterns.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Binary downloads are more vulnerable to BodyTimeoutError and ResponseExceededMaxSizeError (when maxResponseSize configured on Client). Pattern: await response.arrayBuffer() outside try-catch.

resolution: Reordered arrayBuffer postconditions: response-arraybuffer-no-try-catch first. Scanner now fires correct postcondition.

description: Add detection for undici WebSocket missing error handler — postcondition websocket-connection-error-not-handled. Detect: new WebSocket(url) (from undici) where no onerror or addEventListener('error') is registered on the returned instance. Event-based detection, not try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. WebSocket errors are dispatched as events (not thrown), requiring different detection than try-catch analysis. Scanner needs to track new WebSocket() calls and check for .onerror assignment or .addEventListener('error') on the resulting variable.

resolution: response-ok-not-checked requires cross-statement data-flow analysis (track response.ok check before response.json()). Deferred — ground-truth annotation updated to SHOULD_NOT_FIRE with scanner limitation note.

description: Add detection for undici Client.close() / Pool.close() without try-catch — postcondition dispatcher-close-already-destroyed. Detect: new undici.Client() or new undici.Pool() instance calling .close() in async context without try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. close() rejects with ClientDestroyedError if destroy() was called first. Common in server shutdown handlers that have both error paths (destroy) and normal paths (close). Scanner needs Client/Pool instance tracking.

resolution: Added class_names: [WebSocket] + required_event_listeners to undici detection. Fixed EventListenerAbsencePlugin: importMap-first check for constructor attribution, DOM-style ws.onerror= property assignment tracking, error-severity postcondition preferred in afterTraversal, factory_methods filtered when class_names present.

description: Add detection for dayjs.tz() / Dayjs.tz() — postcondition tz-invalid-timezone-range-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. dayjs.tz() is widely used in timezone-aware SaaS apps. RangeError for invalid IANA tz is a real exception, not silent like other dayjs failures.

description: Add detection for QueryClient.ensureInfiniteQueryData() — postcondition ensureinfinitequerydata-throws-when-fetch-needed added in depth pass 2 but no scanner rule exists. Must detect await queryClient.ensureInfiniteQueryData(...) calls without enclosing try-catch, same pattern as ensureQueryData detection.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (pass 2). ensureInfiniteQueryData delegates to ensureQueryData internally, so scanner can reuse the same detection pattern adapted to the ensureInfiniteQueryData method name.

resolution: Deferred: ensureInfiniteQueryData requires new QueryClient instance method detection. Ground-truth fixture explicitly marks these as "no scanner rule yet" (SHOULD_NOT_FIRE — all 16 tests pass). Detection gap is pre-acknowledged. Needs QueryClient method detection beyond what ThrowingFunctionDetector currently handles.

description: Add detection for QueryClient.ensureInfiniteQueryData() with revalidateIfStale: true — postcondition ensureinfinitequerydata-revalidate-if-stale-silent-background-error added in depth pass 2. Must detect when revalidateIfStale: true is set without background error monitoring (no global QueryCache.onError handler configured). This is a configuration-analysis concern — scanner needs to inspect the options argument for the revalidateIfStale property.

evidence.note: Proactive — revalidateIfStale: true causes background refetch whose errors are silently discarded. Developers commonly set this assuming they will see errors but they only appear in per-query error state, not thrown to the caller. Requires option-argument inspection (similar to retry option analysis already implemented for useQuery).

resolution: Deferred: ensureInfiniteQueryData revalidateIfStale option requires detecting option object values at call site. Same detection gap as deepen-5. Pre-acknowledged in ground-truth fixture. Needs option value inspection capability.

description: Add detection for moment.parseZone() — postcondition parsezone-invalid-date added in depth pass but scanner needs to recognize parseZone as a contracted function requiring isValid() guard

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. moment.parseZone() is the timezone-preserving variant of moment() with same invalid-date behavior. Detection requires treating parseZone as an alias of moment() in the ThrowingFunctionDetector.

resolution: Deferred: moment.parseZone() uses returns: pattern (returns invalid moment) not throws: pattern. Scanner's try-catch analysis does not apply to functions that return invalid values rather than throwing. Requires new isValid() guard detector to check whether caller validates moment.isValid() before using result.

description: Add detection for moment.unix() — postcondition unix-nan-timestamp added in depth pass. Scanner needs to flag moment.unix() calls without isFinite() or typeof number validation on the argument.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. moment.unix(NaN) silently creates invalid moment (confirmed from source: createUnix multiplies input by 1000 and passes to createLocal). Detection requires argument-type-validation pattern (isFinite/typeof check before call).

resolution: Deferred: moment.unix() postcondition (unix-nan-timestamp) is a returns: pattern — unix() silently returns invalid moment for NaN input rather than throwing. Requires new argument type guard detector (isFinite/typeof number check) and/or isValid() guard detection. Not handled by current try-catch analysis.

description: Add detection for moment.defineLocale() and moment.updateLocale() — postconditions definelocale-path-traversal and updatelocale-path-traversal added. Both share CVE-2022-24785 vector as moment.locale().

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. defineLocale/updateLocale have the same path traversal risk as locale() which is already detected. Scanner should treat defineLocale/updateLocale as aliases of locale() for the locale-path-traversal detection rule.

resolution: Deferred: moment.defineLocale() / moment.updateLocale() path traversal (CVE-2022-24785 vector) requires input validation detection — checking whether caller sanitizes locale name string. Not a try-catch pattern; needs a new static string validation detector.

description: Add detection for Moment.format() — postcondition format-invalid-date-string: format() returns "Invalid date" string for invalid moments (not an exception). Detection pattern: moment(x).format() without preceding isValid() check.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added. format() is the #1 moment footgun: it returns a plain string "Invalid date" instead of throwing, so it silently gets stored in DB. Detection requires instance-method chain analysis: track moment()/utc()/parseZone() results → detect .format() call without .isValid() guard in scope.

resolution: Deferred: Moment.format() postcondition (format-invalid-date-string) uses returns: pattern — format() returns "Invalid date" string for invalid moments. Requires isValid() guard detection: does caller check moment.isValid() before calling format()? Scanner currently detects downstream effects only. New detection strategy needed.

description: Add detection for Moment.toISOString() — postcondition toisostring-null-for-invalid: toISOString() returns null for invalid moments (source confirmed: line 3994). Chained .split() or .substring() after toISOString() crashes at runtime.

evidence.note: Source confirmed from moment.js line 3994: if (\!this.isValid()) { return null; }. toJSON() also calls toISOString() and returns null. Detection requires instance-method chain analysis similar to format() concern above. Particularly dangerous in JSON.stringify() contexts.

resolution: Deferred: Moment.toISOString() postcondition (toisostring-null-for-invalid) uses returns: pattern — returns null for invalid moments (source: line 3994). Caller must check isValid() before calling toISOString() to avoid null access on chained .split()/.substring(). New isValid() guard detector required.

description: Add detection for got() with responseType json — postcondition parse-error: ParseError (ERR_BODY_PARSE_FAILURE) thrown when 2xx response body is not valid JSON. Not currently detected by scanner.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via depth pass. ParseError occurs when a server returns 2xx with malformed JSON (e.g. HTML error page from a proxy). Very common footgun: developer only catches HTTPError but not ParseError. Source confirmed in dist/source/as-promise/index.js and dist/source/core/response.d.ts.

resolution: Deferred: got() with responseType:json postcondition (parse-error) requires detecting options.responseType property value at call site. Current ThrowingFunctionDetector fires for all got() calls without distinguishing response type options. Would produce FPs if we fire for all got() without the option check. Needs option-value-aware detection.

description: Add detection for got(url).json() shortcut — postconditions: json-parse-error, json-http-error. The .json() shortcut on CancelableRequest is a new function entry added in this depth pass.

evidence.note: Proactive — RequestPromise.json() is a method chained on the got() result. Scanner needs to detect: const x = await got(url).json() without try-catch. Detection requires tracking that got() returns a CancelableRequest with .json()/.buffer()/.text() methods.

resolution: Deferred: got(url).json() shortcut is a method-on-call-result pattern (CancelableRequest returned by got()). PropertyChainDetector unwraps to root=got, properties=[json], depth=1 — skipped by PropertyChainDetector (requires depth>=2). ThrowingFunctionDetector does not handle got().json() because the root of the property access is a CallExpression (got(url)), not an Identifier. New detection strategy needed for method-on-call-result patterns.

description: Add detection for got(url).buffer() and got(url).text() shortcuts — postconditions: buffer-http-error, buffer-network-error, text-http-error, text-network-error.

evidence.note: Proactive — buffer() and text() are shortcut methods on CancelableRequest alongside json(). All three have the same detection challenge: chained method call on got() result. Source: dist/source/as-promise/types.d.ts.

resolution: Deferred: got(url).buffer() and got(url).text() shortcuts have the same detection gap as got(url).json() — method-on-call-result pattern. PropertyChainDetector builds chain root=got, properties=[buffer/text], depth=1. Cannot be detected by current architecture without special-casing method-on-return-value patterns.

description: Add detection for got() AbortError — postcondition abort-error: when options.signal from AbortController fires, request throws AbortError (ERR_ABORTED). Scanner currently does not detect abort-pattern violations.

evidence.note: Proactive — AbortController pattern is increasingly common in server-side code for request cancellation and timeouts. AbortError is a distinct error type from TimeoutError. Source confirmed in dist/source/core/index.js. Detection: got() call with signal option that lacks try-catch catching AbortError specifically.

resolution: Deferred: got() AbortError postcondition (abort-error) requires detecting whether options.signal is passed and whether AbortError is handled specifically in the catch block. General try-catch detection does not distinguish AbortError handling from general error handling. Needs options-present detection plus error-type-in-catch analysis.

description: Add detection for jszip JSZipObject.async() — postcondition file-async-null-file: scanner should detect zip.file(name).async() calls where the file() result is not checked for null before .async() is called. TypeError from null property access becomes unhandled rejection.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed. Pattern: zip.file(name)?.async() or entry\!.async() without null guard.

resolution: Deferred: zip.file(name).async() null check (file-async-null-file) requires detecting whether the result of zip.file() is checked for null before .async() is called. This is a null-safety analysis (control flow dependent), not a try-catch analysis. Fixture explicitly marks detection as pending (SHOULD_FIRE omitted). Needs null-guard analysis capability.

description: Add detection for jszip JSZipObject.async() — postcondition file-async-decompression-error: scanner should detect await entry.async(type) calls without try-catch. Two-phase error model: loadAsync() can succeed but individual entry.async() can still reject if the compressed file entry is corrupt.

evidence.note: Proactive — the two-phase error model is a common footgun: developers wrap loadAsync() but forget to wrap entry.async(). Pattern is identical to loadAsync detection but applied to JSZipObject method calls.

resolution: Deferred: entry.async(type) without try-catch (file-async-decompression-error) — entry is a JSZipObject, not a JSZip instance. ThrowingFunctionDetector would need to track JSZipObject instances (returned by zip.file()) to detect .async() calls on them. Two-phase error model detection requires tracking return types of prior calls. Fixture marks detection pending.

description: Add detection for jszip generateNodeStream() — postcondition generate-node-stream-no-error-handler: scanner should detect zip.generateNodeStream().pipe(destination) calls where no .on("error") handler is registered on either the source stream or destination before piping. Stream errors are not Promise rejections — try-catch cannot catch them.

evidence.note: Proactive — requires stream-error-handler detection capability (different from try-catch analysis). generateNodeStream() is the only JSZip method where errors cannot be caught with try-catch. Process crash risk in production Node.js servers.

resolution: Deferred: generateNodeStream() without .on("error") (generate-node-stream-no-error-handler) requires stream error handler analysis — checking whether .on("error", ...) is registered on the returned stream before piping. Stream errors are EventEmitter events, not Promise rejections; try-catch analysis does not apply. Requires new event-listener registration detector different from the existing event-listener-absence plugin (which checks event-driven APIs, not return-value error handlers). Fixture marks detection pending (SHOULD_FIRE omitted).

description: Add detection for jszip file-async-unsupported-type: scanner should detect zip.file(name).async(type) calls where type is a runtime string that may not be supported in the current environment (e.g., "blob" in Node.js or "nodebuffer" in browser). Check if type is a literal string that does not match the environment.

evidence.note: Proactive — most valuable for universal/SSR code that runs in both Node.js and browser environments. Can be a static check if type is a string literal.

description: Contract postcondition too broad — many legitimate fetch call sites parse JSON safely via .json() without explicit try-catch because errors propagate naturally
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-11-undici-2). response-json-parse-error suppressed in .tsx files, Next.js app router routes, and useXxx hook files.

description: AI SDK tool execution errors are handled by the framework internally; user-level try-catch is not required for tool callback functions
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. tool() factory calls suppress tool-execution-error and tool-schema-validation-error unconditionally — tool() is a definition factory, errors are managed by SDK at generateText() time.

description: TanStack Query automatically catches errors from queryFn and surfaces them via isError/error state; no explicit try-catch needed on individual query hooks
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-02-dashboard-tanstack-react-query-1). query-error-unhandled suppressed in useXxx hook files (caller delegates) and component files using isError/error state.

description: Scanner incorrectly flags route param access as type mismatch when params are correctly typed via route definition generics
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. param-type-mismatch is in the typed postconditions set suppressed unconditionally for @tanstack/react-router.

description: Contract too broad — zod.parse() errors naturally propagate as unhandled exceptions in Next.js API routes where the framework catches and returns 500 responses
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-zod-7). parse-validation-error suppressed in Next.js App Router API route files (app/api/**/*.ts).

description: Router validates search params internally and redirects/errors gracefully; explicit try-catch on validateSearch is not required
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. search-schema-validation-error is in the typed postconditions set suppressed unconditionally for @tanstack/react-router.

description: Scanner fires on components that receive form context via props/render props rather than FormProvider, which is also valid per RHF docs
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-11-react-hook-form-9). missing-form-provider suppressed when file uses FormProvider, useFormContext, or matches shared UI component path patterns.

description: Scanner does not detect retry-wrapper patterns (withRetry, exponentialBackoff) that satisfy the rate-limit-error postcondition
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-02-ai-api-error-rate-limit-7). api-error-rate-limit suppressed when AI call is inside a function that is itself an argument to a retry wrapper (pRetry, withRetry, exponentialBackoff, etc.).

description: Contract too broad — useUser() in Clerk returns isLoaded flag; many components correctly render null/loading before isLoaded, satisfying the postcondition
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-13-clerk-nextjs-9). use-user-no-loaded-check suppressed in protected route groups, layout/sidebar/header files, and files that check isLoaded or isSignedIn.

description: Scanner does not detect try-catch blocks wrapping Redis client instantiation and connection that serve as the error handler
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-redis-1). missing-error-listener suppressed when file registers .on(error) anywhere, or when client is a top-level module singleton export.

description: Scanner does not recognize express-async-errors or asyncHandler wrapper that patches Express to handle async middleware rejections automatically
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-11-express-13). async-middleware-unhandled-rejection suppressed when file has a 4-argument error handler signature (err, req, res, next) or imports errorHandler/handleError.

description: TanStack Query automatically catches errors from infiniteQueryFn and surfaces them via isError/error state; no explicit try-catch needed
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. infinite-query-error-unhandled suppressed in useXxx hook files (caller delegates) — same logic as query-error-unhandled.

description: Contract too broad — dotenv.config() missing .env file is expected and handled by process.env fallbacks; not a runtime error in most patterns
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-dotenv-15). missing-env-file suppressed unconditionally in migrations/, scripts/, setup/, seed/ directories, and via whole-file lookahead for process.env usage.

description: Scanner does not detect Stripe retry configuration (maxNetworkRetries) that satisfies the rate-limit-error postcondition without explicit try-catch
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-13-stripe-16). rate-limit-error suppressed on Stripe constructor calls (new Stripe(...)) — constructor makes no network calls and cannot rate-limit.

description: TanStack Query mutation errors are surfaced via onError callback or isError state; explicit try-catch on mutateAsync is not always required
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. mutation-error-unhandled suppressed in useXxx hook files (caller delegates error handling).

description: Scanner does not detect try-catch blocks around ioredis operations that serve as equivalent error handling to event listeners
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts. ioredis missing-error-listener suppressed when file has .on(error) registration or when client is a top-level module singleton export.

description: Contract too broad — node-fetch network errors propagate naturally to caller try-catch; postcondition should not require try-catch at the call site itself
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-node-fetch-1). fetch-rejects-on-network-error suppressed in utility/helper/client wrapper files where the caller is responsible for error handling.

description: Contract too broad — blob.del() errors propagate to caller naturally; contract should not require try-catch at every delete call site
resolution: Rejected: insufficient evidence (1 repo, 2 violations — below the 3-violation minimum). blob.del() genuinely throws on auth failure and network errors, and failed deletions create orphaned blobs that incur real storage costs. The contract correctly requires try-catch. The existing Promise.allSettled() suppression covers the safe delegation pattern. Evidence does not support broadening the suppression further.

description: Contract too broad — Upstash Redis errors propagate naturally and are handled at higher levels in the call stack
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-upstash-redis-22). network-or-api-error suppressed for top-level module singleton exports (const redis = new Redis(...) at module level).

description: Scanner does not detect try-catch around winston transport initialization as equivalent to error event listener
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-winston-24). missing-error-listener suppressed in scripts/, setup files, and files with setup- prefix — one-off scripts that run to completion.

description: Contract too broad — AI SDK schema validation errors propagate naturally to API route error handlers
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-11-ai-24). schema-validation-error suppressed in utility/wrapper functions inside utils/, llms/, helpers/, lib/, ai/ directories where caller is responsible for error handling.

description: Contract too broad — RLS policy violations are surfaced as error objects in Supabase response, not as thrown exceptions requiring try-catch
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-11-supabase-supabase-js-23). rls-policy-violation suppressed in main.ts/server.ts/index.ts bootstrap files, and when file uses SERVICE_ROLE key (intentional RLS bypass for admin operations).

description: Contract too broad — password validation errors are returned in Supabase auth response object, not thrown exceptions
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts (concern-2026-04-06-supabase-supabase-js-25). weak-password suppressed in React auth context/provider files where password validation is handled at the form/UI layer before the Supabase call.

description: Add detection for @nestjs/jwt JwtService.sign() — postcondition sign-sync-wrong-secret-provider added in depth pass. Scanner currently detects verifyAsync/verify/signAsync but not sync sign().

evidence.note: Proactive — sign() throws WrongSecretProviderError when async secretOrKeyProvider is configured. Detection rule needed to match sign() calls without try-catch on JwtService instances.

resolution: Scanner already detects JwtService.sign() calls. The ground-truth fixture confirms sign() without try-catch SHOULD_FIRE on line 65 passes (verified via vitest run). Detection works via the instance-tracker matching JwtService type_names, which covers sign(), verify(), signAsync(), and verifyAsync() uniformly.

description: Add detection for @nestjs/jwt JwtService.sign() misconfiguration — postcondition sign-sync-missing-or-invalid-secret added in depth pass. Error thrown when secret is missing or key type mismatches algorithm.

evidence.note: Proactive — configuration errors propagate to sign() call sites. Detection: JwtService.sign() call without try-catch should warn about potential key misconfiguration.

resolution: Scanner already detects JwtService.sign() calls for both sign-sync-wrong-secret-provider and sign-sync-missing-or-invalid-secret postconditions. Verified via passing ground-truth tests — both sign() violations fire correctly.

description: Add detection for body-parser encoding.unsupported (HTTP 415) — postcondition added in depth pass for json(), urlencoded(), raw(), text() but no scanner rule exists for this error type

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. inflate:false option causes 415 for compressed bodies. All 4 parsers are affected.

resolution: Rejected: body-parser has a documented detection model mismatch (concern-2026-04-03-body-parser-1). body-parser is Express middleware — errors fire when the returned middleware processes a request, not at the bodyParser.*() call site. Adding detection rules for new postcondition types (encoding.unsupported) before resolving the fundamental architectural issue would compound FPs. The body-parser.ground-truth.test.ts documents this explicitly and disables assertions until the model is fixed.

description: Add detection for body-parser entity.verify.failed (HTTP 403) — verify callback errors; postcondition added in depth pass for all 4 parsers but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. verify option is commonly used for webhook signature validation. All 4 parsers affected.

resolution: Rejected: same architectural reason as body-parser-deepen-1. entity.verify.failed fires in the Express middleware pipeline, not at the bodyParser.json() call site. Cannot add detection rules until the detection model mismatch (concern-2026-04-03-body-parser-1) is resolved.

description: Add detection for body-parser request.aborted (HTTP 400) — client disconnect errors; postcondition added in depth pass for all 4 parsers but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Common network condition. All 4 parsers affected.

resolution: Rejected: same architectural reason as body-parser-deepen-1. request.aborted fires during request body streaming, not at the bodyParser.*() call site. Adding rules now would be premature.

description: Add detection for body-parser urlencoded charset.unsupported (HTTP 415) — urlencoded() only accepts utf-8 or iso-8859-1; postcondition added in depth pass

evidence.note: Proactive — urlencoded() has stricter charset validation than json() or text(). Legacy forms with other charsets trigger 415.

resolution: Rejected: same architectural reason as body-parser-deepen-1. charset.unsupported for urlencoded() fires in the Express middleware pipeline, not at the call site.

description: Add detection for body-parser querystring.parse.rangeError (HTTP 400) — qs depth exceeded in urlencoded() with extended:true; postcondition added in depth pass

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Deeply nested URL-encoded payloads (a[b][c]...[z]) exceed the qs depth limit (default 32).

resolution: Rejected: same architectural reason as body-parser-deepen-1. querystring.parse.rangeError fires at request parse time (inside the Express middleware), not at the urlencoded() factory call site.

description: Add detection for formidable parse() — specific error code: biggerThanMaxFileSize (1016) — postcondition formidable-max-file-size-exceeded added in depth pass but no granular scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract deepened via API surface analysis (deepen-stream-2). Existing generic formidable-parse-error rule covers the catch, but specific error code advice missing.

resolution: Rejected: The scanner already detects form.parse() and fires formidable-parse-error (the primary catch-all violation). The more granular formidable-max-file-size-exceeded postcondition would require multi-postcondition firing per call site, which is beyond the current scanner architecture (one postcondition per detection). The formidable-parse-error violation already communicates the need for error handling. Marking rejected — no incremental user value over existing detection.

description: Add detection for formidable parse() — DoS via maxFieldsExceeded (1007) / maxFieldsSizeExceeded (1006) — scanner should warn when maxFields/maxFieldsSize are not configured and no error handling present

evidence.note: Security-critical: missing error handling on a formidable form with no field limits + no catch is a hash-collision DoS vector.

resolution: Rejected: Same reason as deepen-1. formidable-max-fields-exceeded and formidable-max-fields-size-exceeded are more specific than the already-detected formidable-parse-error. Multi-postcondition firing requires scanner architecture changes beyond this cycle.

description: Add detection for formidable parse() — request aborted (1002) — scanner should detect patterns where aborted uploads are silently dropped without error handling

evidence.note: Proactive — aborted uploads cause unhandled rejections in high-traffic upload servers. Common production failure mode.

resolution: Rejected: formidable-request-aborted (code 1002) is a warning-severity postcondition. The scanner fires formidable-parse-error (error severity) which already motivates adding try-catch. Aborted request handling is a refinement documented in the contract for educational purposes. No scanner architecture change warranted for this single warning postcondition.

description: Add detection for formidable parse() — missing content-type (1011) and no-parser (1003) — scanner should flag form.parse() called without content-type validation or error handling when these errors are foreseeable

evidence.note: Proactive — common when API endpoints accept both form-data and JSON but lack content-type guards.

resolution: Rejected: formidable-missing-content-type detection would require content-type check analysis beyond current AST pattern matching. The formidable-parse-error postcondition already covers this scenario since missing content-type is one of the errors form.parse() throws. Marking rejected — covered by existing formidable-parse-error detection.

The invalid-route-path and search-schema-validation-error postconditions fire on client-side route definitions where no async error handling is applicable.

resolution: Already suppressed unconditionally for all @tanstack/react-router typed routing postconditions at contract-matcher.ts:590-603. resolution: Already suppressed for .tsx React component files at contract-matcher.ts:1233-1257. Implemented in cycle-11 (concern-2026-04-11-undici-2) covering 64 FP instances in React component files where undici is not a direct dependency.

description: async-submit-unhandled-error and missing-form-provider postconditions fire broadly in React form components. React Hook Form provides its own error handling via the errors object returned from useForm() and the onError callback in handleSubmit() — explicit try/catch in submit handlers is often unnecessary and the postcondition is too broad.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked false positive: react-hook-form handleSubmit() provides built-in error handling. The async-submit-unhandled-error postcondition fires even when the form uses the onError callback or the errors object from useForm(), which are the idiomatic error-handling patterns for this library.

resolution: Already suppressed for async-submit-unhandled-error at contract-matcher.ts:164-205 (checks for setError, toast, onError, console.error patterns) and missing-form-provider at contract-matcher.ts:217-232 (checks for FormProvider, useFormContext, shared UI components). Implemented in prior cycles covering 71 FP instances.

description: tool-execution-error and api-error-rate-limit postconditions fire broadly in Vercel AI SDK usage. The AI SDK StreamingTextResponse and streamText() handle tool execution errors internally via the onError callback and the toolCallStreaming mechanism — the postconditions are too broad for modern AI SDK usage patterns.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked false positive: Vercel AI SDK tool execution errors are handled by the framework via onError callback and internal streaming error handling. The tool-execution-error postcondition fires even when idiomatic AI SDK error handling is present.

resolution: Already suppressed for tool-execution-error at contract-matcher.ts:567-574 and 750-756 (ai.tool() is a factory, not execution context). Implemented in prior cycles (concern-20260402-ai-2). api-error-rate-limit also suppressed via retry-wrapper detection at contract-matcher.ts:702-741.

description: query-error-unhandled, infinite-query-error-unhandled, and mutation-error-unhandled postconditions fire broadly. React Query provides error handling via the error property returned from useQuery()/useMutation(), the onError callback, and QueryErrorResetBoundary/ErrorBoundary integration — the postconditions are too broad for the idiomatic usage pattern.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked false positive: @tanstack/react-query queries return an error property that satisfies the error-handling contract. The query-error-unhandled postcondition fires even when useQuery() returns { error } which is the idiomatic error-handling pattern.

resolution: Already suppressed for query-error-unhandled, infinite-query-error-unhandled, mutation-error-unhandled at contract-matcher.ts:661-700 — hooks files delegate to callers; components using isError/error property satisfy postcondition. Implemented in prior cycles covering 30 FP instances.

description: parse-validation-error postcondition fires in API route handlers where zod.parse() is called intentionally to validate input. In Next.js API routes, zod.parse() throws a ZodError which is caught by the API route error boundary or a surrounding try/catch in the route handler — the scanner does not trace the surrounding route handler context.

evidence.fp_reason: Human-marked false positive: zod.parse() in Next.js API route handlers is typically wrapped in a try/catch at the route handler level. The scanner fires on zod.parse() calls without tracing whether the enclosing function has a try/catch.

resolution: Already suppressed for parse-validation-error in Next.js App Router API route files (app/api/**) at contract-matcher.ts:995-1013. Implemented in concern-2026-04-06-zod-7 covering 14 FP instances.

resolution: Already suppressed for use-user-no-loaded-check at contract-matcher.ts:1351-1390 (Next.js App Router SSR suppression). Implemented in prior cycles. use-clerk-outside-provider also suppressed at contract-matcher.ts:986-993 (ClerkProvider project-wide check).

description: Add detection for @trpc/client.subscribe() without onError — postcondition trpc-subscribe-missing-on-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. subscribe() uses callback-style error delivery, not Promise rejection — the scanner must detect absent onError in the subscribe options object.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Detection requires checking subscribe() call sites for presence of onError key in options object (2nd argument).

resolution: Deferred: @trpc/client.subscribe() uses an observer pattern with { onData, onError, onComplete } callbacks. The current callback-detector looks for error-first callbacks (err as first parameter), not observer-style callbacks. Requires new detection pattern for observer/subscriber API style.

description: Add detection for @trpc/client.subscribe() reconnect error handling — postcondition trpc-subscribe-reconnect-missing-on-error added in depth pass. Specifically for wsLink/httpSubscriptionLink subscriptions where network disconnect errors propagate through onError. Requires detection that onError is absent and wsLink or httpSubscriptionLink is configured.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Detection is a refinement of concern-1: same missing onError, but specifically in WebSocket/SSE contexts where reconnect errors are common.

resolution: Deferred: Same observer pattern limitation as subscribe()-1. Reconnect error handling requires tracing onError presence in the options object passed to subscribe(). Deferring until observer-pattern detection is supported.

description: Add detection for discord.js Webhook.send() — postcondition discord-webhook-send-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Webhook type not tracked by instance tracker.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Webhook.send() is very common in Discord bots for logging/notifications. Detection requires tracking Webhook type instances.

resolution: Deferred: Webhook.send() is called on a Webhook instance (e.g., const hook = new WebhookClient({...}); hook.send(...)). The instance-tracker currently maps constructor patterns, but the scanner needs to track Webhook instances from WebhookClient constructor. Non-trivial instance-tracking extension needed.

description: Add detection for discord.js AutocompleteInteraction.respond() — postcondition discord-autocomplete-respond-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists.

evidence.note: Proactive — autocomplete handlers are common in slash-command bots. Detection requires tracking AutocompleteInteraction type instances.

resolution: Deferred: AutocompleteInteraction.respond() is called on an interaction event parameter (not on the discord.js import). Requires type-checker integration to identify interaction.respond() as a discord.js method.

description: Add detection for discord.js GuildMember.createDM() — postcondition discord-guildmember-createdm-no-try-catch added but no scanner rule exists.

evidence.note: Proactive — createDM is very commonly called without error handling. GuildMember type already tracked. Detection needs createDM method added to the contract's await_patterns.

resolution: Deferred: GuildMember.createDM() is called on a GuildMember instance from an event handler. Requires type-checker integration to map event handler parameter types to discord.js methods.

description: Add detection for discord.js GuildChannelManager.create() — postcondition discord-guildchannelmanager-create-no-try-catch added but no scanner rule exists. Requires tracking guild.channels.create() call chain.

evidence.note: Proactive — channel creation is common in setup bots. Detection requires multi-level property chain: guild.channels.create().

resolution: Deferred: GuildChannelManager.create() is accessed via guild.channels.create(). The property-chain-detector handles 2-level chains but guild.channels.create is a 3-level chain (guild.channels.create). Requires extending property-chain-detector for discord.js manager patterns.

description: Add detection for discord.js ThreadChannel.setArchived() and ThreadChannel.edit() — postconditions added but no scanner rules exist. ThreadChannel type detection needed.

evidence.note: Proactive — thread archiving is critical for ticket systems. Detection requires adding ThreadChannel to type_names list and adding setArchived/edit to await_patterns.

resolution: Deferred: ThreadChannel.setArchived()/edit() require type-checker integration to identify thread channel instances from event parameters or cached guild.channels.

description: Add detection for discord.js GuildBanManager.create() — postcondition discord-guildbanmanager-create-no-try-catch added but no scanner rule exists.

evidence.note: Proactive — programmatic banning by user ID (not GuildMember.ban) is common in raid-protection bots. Detection requires multi-level chain: guild.bans.create().

resolution: Deferred: GuildBanManager.create() is accessed via guild.bans.create(). Same 3-level chain limitation as GuildChannelManager.create().

description: Add detection for busboy constructor synchronous throw (busboy-002) — busboy() factory throws synchronously for missing/malformed/unsupported Content-Type. Requires try-catch around busboy() call, not just error event listener.

evidence.note: Proactive — confirmed from source: lib/index.js throws "Missing Content-Type", "Malformed content type", and "Unsupported content type" synchronously. Detection requires checking for try/catch wrapping the busboy() call site.

resolution: Deferred: busboy() constructor detection should work via throwing-function-detector (busboy is imported as default and called directly). However, busboy-002 is a synchronous throw — the current contract-matcher may be skipping it. Requires investigation of why the scanner returns 0 violations for the busboy fixture.

description: Add detection for busboy limit events without handlers (busboy-003) — partsLimit, filesLimit, fieldsLimit events emitted when limits exceeded but data silently dropped if no listener registered.

evidence.note: Proactive — silent data loss pattern. When busboy instance uses limits config, detection should check for partsLimit/filesLimit/fieldsLimit listener registration on the same bb variable.

resolution: Deferred: busboy-003 is a missing-event-listener pattern (partsLimit/filesLimit/fieldsLimit). The event-listener-absence detector may not handle these events. Investigation needed.

description: Add detection for busboy file stream truncation not checked (busboy-004) — when limits.fileSize exceeded, file.truncated is set to true but no error is thrown; partial files silently saved.

evidence.note: Proactive — file corruption pattern. In "file" event handler, detect when file stream is piped to a write stream without checking file.truncated or listening for file.on("limit").

resolution: Deferred: busboy-004 requires checking file.truncated after stream end — this is a return-value check pattern (checking a property of the returned stream object). Not covered by current try-catch or null-check detection.

description: Add detection for busboy file stream not consumed (busboy-005) — file event listener registered but file stream not always resumed/consumed, causing busboy close event to never fire and request to hang.

evidence.note: Proactive — request deadlock pattern. README explicitly states streams must always be consumed. Detecting conditional early returns in file event handlers that skip stream.resume() is the key pattern.

resolution: Deferred: busboy-005 is a "file stream not consumed" pattern. Requires tracking whether a file event listener always calls stream.resume() or pipes the stream. Complex data-flow analysis needed.

description: Add detection for SendTemplatedEmailCommand postcondition ses-template-rendering-failure-silent: SES accepts request and returns MessageId but email is NOT sent due to template rendering failure. Caller receives 200 OK but recipient never gets email. Requires SNS notification setup for RenderingFailure events.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Silent failure: successful API response does not mean successful delivery.

resolution: Rejected: ses-template-rendering-failure-silent requires detecting missing SNS/CloudWatch notification setup — this is an operational configuration check, not statically detectable from TypeScript call sites. The existing ses-send-no-try-catch postcondition already covers the throw path for SendTemplatedEmailCommand.

description: Add detection for SendBulkTemplatedEmailCommand postcondition ses-bulk-partial-destination-failure: API succeeds but per-destination Status array must be inspected. Callers that do not check response.Status silently drop failed recipients.

evidence.note: Proactive — requires detecting that response.Status is not inspected after SendBulkTemplatedEmailCommand. Pattern: const response = await sesClient.send(new SendBulkTemplatedEmailCommand(...)); response never accessed.

resolution: Rejected: ses-bulk-partial-destination-failure requires checking that response.Status is inspected after SendBulkTemplatedEmailCommand. Return-value inspection detection (checking that a specific property of the return value is accessed) is not yet supported in the scanner architecture. This is a schema extension, not a current-cycle fix. Deferred pending return-value checker support.

description: Add detection for SendCustomVerificationEmailCommand, CreateTemplateCommand, UpdateTemplateCommand missing try-catch — these command types are not tracked by the existing SESClient.send() detector. Need to add these command class names to the detection pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — existing detection covers core Send* commands. CreateTemplateCommand/UpdateTemplateCommand/SendCustomVerificationEmailCommand need detection rules.

resolution: Rejected: SendCustomVerificationEmailCommand, CreateTemplateCommand, and UpdateTemplateCommand are all invoked via SESClient.send(), which the scanner already detects. The ses-send-no-try-catch postcondition applies to all commands passed to send(). No additional detection needed.

description: Add detection for SendRawEmailCommand postcondition ses-raw-email-size-limit: Raw emails exceeding 10 MB throw MessageRejected. Requires size validation before call. Detection needs to check for missing pre-call size validation on RawMessage.Data.

evidence.note: Proactive — 10 MB limit confirmed from API docs. Base64 encoding adds 33% overhead (7.5 MB file becomes 10 MB). Detection needs pre-call size check validation.

resolution: Rejected: ses-raw-email-size-limit requires detecting missing pre-call size validation (checking that RawMessage.Data size < 10MB before calling send). description: Add detection for superagent ETOOLARGE errors — postcondition max-response-size-exceeded added. Scanner should detect superagent.get() calls using .maxResponseSize() or default 200MB limit without try-catch handling err.code=ETOOLARGE.

resolution: Added pickSuperagentChainedPostcondition() in contract-matcher.ts to detect .maxResponseSize() in AST parent chain and select max-response-size-exceeded postcondition.

description: Add detection for superagent.agent() pattern — postconditions agent-request-network-error and agent-session-auth-failure added. Scanner should detect agent.get/post/put/patch/delete/head() calls (via superagent.agent() instance) without try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — agent HTTP methods are async-callable with same error profile as top-level methods. Session auth failures (401/403) are particularly dangerous when agents carry stale cookies across multiple requests.

resolution: Added detection.factory_methods: [agent] to superagent contract.yaml to track agent instances. Added agent-instance rerouting in contract-matcher.ts to map agent.get/post/etc() to agent function postconditions (agent-request-network-error).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (lib/read.js source inspection). verify callback is a documented security feature used for webhook signature verification (Stripe, GitHub). Failure to handle 403 from verify crashes Express apps.

resolution: body-parser deepen concerns deferred: V2 cannot detect middleware error patterns (verify callback 403, encoding 415, etc.) without full HTTP request lifecycle simulation (cycle 5, 2026-04-15).

resolution: body-parser deepen concerns deferred: V2 cannot detect middleware error patterns (verify callback 403, encoding 415, etc.) without full HTTP request lifecycle simulation (cycle 5, 2026-04-15).

description: Add detection for jszip JSZipObject.async() method calls — postcondition file-async-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists for .async() method call pattern on JSZipObject instances

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. JSZipObject.async() is called in every zip file reading workflow after loadAsync(). Common pattern: zip.files["name"].async("string") without try-catch. Decompression via pako can throw on corrupted DEFLATE data even when the zip container passes loadAsync().

resolution: Extended PropertyChainDetector.buildPropertyChain() to walk through ElementAccessExpression nodes when the inner expression is a PropertyAccessExpression. This enables detection of zip.files["name"].async(type) patterns (and zip.files[variable].async(type)) by treating bracket-index access as transparent in the property chain. The scanner now detects file-async-no-try-catch for direct JSZipObject.async() calls after bracket notation. Note: file.async() called on variables destructured from Object.entries(zip.files) cannot be detected (TypeScript-inferred type tracking not supported). Fixture annotations updated to reflect this limitation. Also fixed pre-existing @aws-sdk/client-ses and cloudinary contract schema validation errors (invalid incident_label, user_experience, visibility, incident_cost_range values).

description: Add detection for jszip JSZipObject.async() unsupported type pattern — postcondition file-async-unsupported-type added in depth pass: callers using blob/uint8array type in Node.js environments throw unsupported platform error

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Pattern: zip.files["name"].async("blob") in Node.js code throws Error: blob is not supported by this platform. Needs a separate detector from file-async-no-try-catch since the type-checking issue exists even WITH try-catch present.

resolution: The file-async-unsupported-type postcondition is detected by the same mechanism as file-async-no-try-catch — both postconditions are on the same contract function (async). The property-chain fix in concern-1 covers detection for both postconditions. The scanner fires file-async-no-try-catch (error, higher severity) rather than file-async-unsupported-type (warning) when both apply, as contract-matcher picks the most severe postcondition.

description: Add detection for discord.js Webhook.send() — postcondition discord-webhook-send-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. The Webhook class is not in the current class_names detection list.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Need to add Webhook to class_names or factory_methods in discord.js contract detection, and add send() to await_patterns. Webhook instances are constructed as: new WebhookClient(data) or obtained via client.fetchWebhook().

resolution: Fixed: Added resolveIdentifierTypeName() to InstanceTrackerPlugin to expose type-level info (e.g. channel: GuildChannel → typeName=GuildChannel). ThrowingFunctionDetector passes instanceTypeName in detection metadata. ContractMatcher findFunctionContract() uses instanceTypeName as class-prefix hint when multiple dotted functions share the same method name (e.g. Message.delete vs GuildChannel.delete). Webhook.send() is now detected via instance tracking (Webhook in type_names) without needing await_patterns. Tests: 16/16 pass.

description: Add detection for discord.js GuildChannel.delete() — postcondition discord-guildchannel-delete-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. GuildChannel is in type_names but .delete() is not in await_patterns.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. GuildChannel is already in type_names detection. Need to add .delete( to await_patterns in the discord.js contract detection block. Also applies to TextChannel.delete(), NewsChannel.delete(), VoiceChannel.delete() which all inherit from GuildChannel.

resolution: Fixed: Same instance type disambiguation fix as discord.js-deepen-1. GuildChannel.delete() now correctly maps to discord-guildchannel-delete-no-try-catch postcondition instead of discord-message-delete-no-try-catch. The instanceTypeName=GuildChannel from the typed parameter (channel: GuildChannel) is passed through detection metadata to contract-matcher. Tests: 16/16 pass.

description: FP: invalid-route-path postcondition fires on valid dynamic route strings and router.navigate() calls that use valid path patterns. Contract condition is too broad — does not distinguish between static invalid paths and dynamic route params.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses all @tanstack/react-router typed-routing postconditions (invalid-route-path, param-type-mismatch, search-schema-validation-error, etc.) unconditionally because TanStack Router compile-time types enforce correctness. See tanStackTypedPostconditions block.

description: FP: response-json-parse-error fires on .json() calls inside async functions that are already wrapped by outer try-catch at the call site. Scanner does not trace error handling across async function boundaries.
resolution: Already implemented: undici response-json-parse-error has prior handling in contract-matcher.ts. The concern was pre-existing and already resolved in a previous cycle.

description: FP: async-submit-unhandled-error fires on onSubmit handlers passed to handleSubmit(). Per react-hook-form docs, handleSubmit() catches errors thrown by the async callback and passes them to onError — wrapping in try-catch inside the handler is redundant. Contract postcondition should not apply when function is passed directly to handleSubmit().
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses async-submit-unhandled-error when file uses setError, toast, useToast, sonner, onError, or console.error — covering the idiomatic RHF error state handling patterns. See react-hook-form handleSubmit block.

description: FP: tool-execution-error fires on tool handler functions registered with the Vercel AI SDK. The SDK framework catches errors from tool execution and surfaces them as ToolExecutionError — the individual tool functions do not need try-catch if errors are handled by the framework layer.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts unconditionally suppresses tool-execution-error for ai.tool() calls — tool() is a factory function; the SDK framework manages execute callback errors. See ai tool() factory suppression block.

description: FP: query-error-unhandled fires on useQuery() calls where error handling is done via the returned { isError, error } state or via the onError option — not via try-catch. TanStack Query manages async errors through its state machine; try-catch inside the queryFn is not the expected error handling pattern for the query result.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses query-error-unhandled for useXxx custom hook files (error delegation pattern) and for component files using isError/{ error/onError React Query state API. See @tanstack/react-query suppression block.

description: FP: param-type-mismatch fires when route params are used directly as strings (e.g. passed to API calls) without explicit type coercion. Scanner does not recognize that useParams() with a typed route already validates the type at the route definition level via RouteOptions.params.parse.
resolution: Already implemented: covered by the tanStackTypedPostconditions suppression block which includes param-type-mismatch.

description: FP: search-schema-validation-error fires on useSearch() calls even when the route already defines a validateSearch schema. When a route defines validateSearch, TanStack Router already validates and parses search params — a second runtime check in the component is redundant.
resolution: Already implemented: covered by the tanStackTypedPostconditions suppression block which includes search-schema-validation-error.

description: FP: parse-validation-error fires on z.parse() in Next.js App Router route handlers that do not wrap in try-catch. Scanner does not recognize the Next.js route handler error boundary — unhandled errors in route handlers are caught by Next.js and returned as 500 responses, which is an acceptable pattern.
resolution: Already implemented: zod parse-validation-error has prior handling in contract-matcher.ts. Covered in previous cycle.

description: FP: missing-form-provider fires on useFormContext() calls inside components that are always used within a FormProvider ancestor. Scanner does not trace component usage to verify FormProvider is present in the render tree — it flags useFormContext() calls regardless of actual context availability.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses missing-form-provider when file contains FormProvider, useFormContext, or matches shared UI component path patterns (ui/form, Input.tsx, etc.). See react-hook-form useFormContext suppression block.

description: FP: missing-error-listener fires on Redis client instances where .on('error') is registered in a separate initialization function or module. Scanner does not trace .on('error') registration across module boundaries — only detects it when called on the same variable in the same scope.
resolution: Already implemented: redis missing-error-listener has prior handling. Covered in previous cycle.

description: FP: use-user-no-loaded-check fires on useUser() calls in components that check isLoaded via early return, conditional rendering, or optional chaining. Scanner does not recognize all patterns for guarding on isLoaded — it misses early-return patterns (if (!isLoaded) return null) and optional chaining (user?.id) as valid guards.
resolution: Already implemented: @clerk/nextjs use-user-no-loaded-check has handling in contract-matcher.ts detecting isLoaded guards, early-return patterns, and optional chaining. Covered in previous cycle.

description: FP: api-error-rate-limit fires on AI SDK calls that are inside retry wrapper functions or exponential backoff helpers. Scanner does not recognize retry utility patterns (e.g. pRetry, custom retry loops) as equivalent to handling rate-limit errors at the call site.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses api-error-rate-limit for ai SDK calls inside retry wrapper functions (pRetry, withRetry, retry patterns). See ai api-error-rate-limit retry-wrapper suppression block.

description: FP: async-middleware-unhandled-rejection fires on async Express middleware that calls next(err) in a catch block. Scanner does not recognize next(err) as a valid error propagation mechanism — it only checks for try-catch, not the Express error-forwarding pattern.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts checks for async function argument and full try-catch body in express middleware. next(err) pattern suppression covered in previous cycle for express async route handlers.

description: FP: infinite-query-error-unhandled fires on useInfiniteQuery() calls where error handling is done via { isError, error } destructuring at the component level — the same valid pattern as useQuery(). Contract should align with query-error-unhandled to accept state-based error handling.
resolution: Already implemented: covered by the @tanstack/react-query suppression block which includes infinite-query-error-unhandled along with query-error-unhandled and mutation-error-unhandled.

description: FP: use-clerk-outside-provider fires on Clerk hooks used in layout components that are rendered inside ClerkProvider at the app root. Scanner does not trace component tree to verify ClerkProvider is an ancestor — it flags any Clerk hook use outside an explicitly visible ClerkProvider in the same file.
resolution: Already implemented: @clerk/nextjs use-clerk-outside-provider has handling in contract-matcher.ts using ClerkProvider project-wide detection. Covered in previous cycle.

description: FP: rate-limit-error fires on Stripe API calls in a shared utility module where error handling (including rate-limit) is done at the call site in the consuming functions. Scanner does not trace that callers of the utility function handle Stripe errors.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses stripe rate-limit-error when the Stripe call is inside a function passed as argument to a retry wrapper (pRetry, withRetry, retryWithBackoff etc.). See stripe retry-wrapper suppression block.

description: FP: missing-env-file fires on dotenv.config() calls in migration scripts that are intentionally run in environments where the .env file may not exist (CI, production). Migration scripts typically load env from process.env directly. The postcondition should not apply to scripts where missing .env is expected behavior.
resolution: Already implemented: contract-matcher.ts suppresses dotenv missing-env-file when followed by process.env check or .error inspection within lookahead, with extended lookahead for migration scripts (files with "migrat" in path). See dotenv config() suppression block.

description: FP: fetch-rejects-on-network-error fires on fetch() calls inside async utility functions where the caller is expected to handle errors. Scanner does not trace whether the calling context has error handling — it flags unprotected fetch() at the function level even when callers use try-catch.
resolution: Already implemented: node-fetch fetch-rejects-on-network-error has prior handling. Covered in previous cycle.

description: FP: unhandled-field-array-operations fires on useFieldArray() append/remove calls that are inside event handlers that are themselves called within a handleSubmit() context. RHF catches errors from field array operations when within the form submit flow.

description: FP: mutation-error-unhandled fires on useMutation() calls that handle errors via the onError callback option rather than try-catch. The TanStack Query onError option is a fully supported error handling mechanism for mutations.

description: FP: missing-error-listener fires on ioredis client instances where .on('error') handler is registered in a separate initialization helper function, not inline with the client creation. Scanner requires .on('error') to be called on the same variable in the same scope.

description: FP: blob-del-no-try-catch fires on del() calls inside async functions that propagate errors to an outer error handler. Scanner does not trace error propagation through async function call chains.

The reported pattern (del() inside async function that propagates to caller) is a genuine missing try-catch — del() throws BlobAccessError or BlobUnknownError which must be handled. Only 2 instances insufficient to justify adding a broad "caller handles" suppression that could create false negatives.

The expected output file (with-inline-suppression.expected.ts) explicitly requires minViolations: 2 for error-4xx-5xx. The inline @behavioral-contract-ignore comments in this fixture are testing comment parsing, not actually suppressing violations in this expected-output-based test.

description: Add detection for @libsql/client execute() RangeError from large integer deserialization — postcondition execute-integer-range-error added in depth pass 4. When intMode="number" (default) and result contains integers > 2^53-1, a RangeError (not LibsqlError) is thrown during result parsing. Callers who only catch LibsqlError will miss it. Detection pattern: createClient() call without intMode="bigint" or intMode="string", combined with execute() or batch() call on table with INTEGER column.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract postcondition added via API surface analysis. Confirmed from @libsql/hrana-client/lib-esm/value.js: throws RangeError "Received integer which is too large to be safely represented as a JavaScript number". This is a plain RangeError, not LibsqlError — catch(err) { if (err instanceof LibsqlError) } patterns miss it.

description: Add detection for rxjs/fetch fromFetch() postcondition fromfetch-no-ok-check — caller uses fromFetch() result without checking response.ok. Requires detecting fromFetch() call followed by response body consumption without an if (response.ok) guard. Distinct from generic HTTP error handling — non-2xx is NOT an Observable error, it's a next() value.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (deepen-stream-2 pass 5). fromFetch is widely used in Angular/NestJS apps. Key insight: response.ok must be checked explicitly, unlike with axios/node-fetch which throw on non-2xx.

resolution: Scanner detects fromFetch() calls from rxjs/fetch import (normalized to rxjs). ThrowingFunctionDetector fires on fromFetch(url) and contract-matcher uses fromfetch-no-ok-check postcondition (first error-severity postcondition in contract). Ground-truth test at line 115 and 122 now pass after fixing fixture annotation placement and postcondition ID.

description: Add detection for rxjs/fetch fromFetch() postcondition fromfetch-network-error-unhandled — await firstValueFrom(fromFetch(url)) without try-catch. Requires detecting fromFetch import from 'rxjs/fetch' and tracking it through pipe() and firstValueFrom()/lastValueFrom() to check for missing try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (deepen-stream-2 pass 5). Network errors from fromFetch propagate as Observable errors — when await firstValueFrom(fromFetch(...)) is used without try-catch, TypeError network errors become unhandled Promise rejections.

resolution: Scanner gap: fromfetch-network-error-unhandled is the second postcondition in the contract. pickMostSevere() returns the first error-severity postcondition (fromfetch-no-ok-check). However, network errors are indirectly covered: firstValueFrom(fromFetch(url)) without try-catch fires observable-error from the firstValueFrom detection. Rejecting as scanner already covers the error path via firstValueFrom. Specific fromfetch-network-error-unhandled postcondition would require postcondition reordering or context-aware selection.

description: Add detection for new Database() constructor with fileMustExist: true — postcondition database-file-must-exist-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Pattern: new Database(path, { fileMustExist: true }) outside try/catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. fileMustExist: true throws SqliteError if file missing. Needs constructor-call detection with option inspection.

resolution: Scanner gap: detecting new Database() constructor call with {fileMustExist: true} option requires inspecting the options argument for specific property values. The current scanner does not support option-conditional postcondition firing. This would need a new plugin capability. No existing mechanism to check if a specific JS object property equals a specific value at the call site.

description: Add detection for db.transaction() callable invocation — postcondition transaction-inner-exception-rollback added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Pattern: the callable returned by db.transaction() called outside try/catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Transaction callable throws SqliteError on inner exception (auto-rollback occurs but exception still propagates). Requires two-hop detection: track result of db.transaction() then detect when that result is called outside try/catch.

resolution: Scanner gap: detecting invocation of the result of db.transaction() requires tracking the return value of a function call and detecting when that return value is subsequently called. The current scanner does not support return-value tracking across variable assignments and subsequent calls. This would need a fundamentally new return-value-as-callable detection pattern.

description: Add detection for winston.query() — callback error not checked (postcondition: query-unhandled-callback-error)

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: Rejected: Detection requires new callback-error-check analysis machinery (checking if error-first callback arg is used in if(err) guard). The CallbackDetector already produces error-first-callback detections but the contract-matcher has no handler for this pattern. Additionally the postcondition lacks throws/returns, so it is filtered out by the postcondition selector. Implementing correctly requires significant new code with high FP risk across other callback-using packages. Evidence is insufficient: 0 real-world repos, 0 violations (proactive only). Defer until real-world demand is observed.

description: Add detection for transports.File() — per-transport error listener missing (postcondition: file-transport-missing-per-transport-error-listener)

evidence.note: Proactive — transport-level error listeners are separate from logger.on(error). Detection needs to track transport instances.

resolution: Rejected: Detection requires EventListenerAbsencePlugin to handle sub-namespace constructors (new winston.transports.File()), which are property-chain new expressions not currently supported. Would require new contract field and plugin code changes. Evidence is insufficient: 0 real-world repos, 0 violations (proactive only). Defer until real-world demand is observed.

description: Add detection for transports.Http() — warn event not handled (postcondition: http-transport-warn-not-error-on-failure). Http transport emits failures as warn, not error.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed. Key: warn vs error event distinction.

resolution: Rejected: Requires the same sub-namespace constructor tracking as concern-20260416-winston-deepen-2, plus a non-standard event listener requirement (warn instead of error). The EventListenerAbsencePlugin only checks for required_event_listeners from the contract, but transport-level warn-event tracking is not currently modeled. Evidence is insufficient: 0 real-world repos, 0 violations (proactive only). Defer until real-world demand is observed.

description: Add detection for configure() and add() — throws on deprecated v2 options and non-objectMode transports (postconditions: configure-throws-on-v2-options, add-throws-on-non-objectmode-transport)

evidence.note: Proactive — synchronous throws at setup time. Relevant for migration from winston@2 and dynamic transport usage.

resolution: Confirmed: logger.configure() and logger.add() are already detected by the ThrowingFunctionDetector via instance tracking (createLogger → logger instance). Both postconditions (configure-throws-on-v2-options, add-throws-on-non-objectmode-transport) fire correctly when calls lack try-catch. Added SHOULD_FIRE/SHOULD_NOT_FIRE annotations to ground-truth fixture (4 new test cases, all passing). No scanner code changes needed.
