description: Add detection for Db.createCollection() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await db.createCollection() without try-catch. Needs Db instance tracking (db = client.db(...) pattern) plus createcollection await_pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. createCollection() used in startup/migration code without error handling is a common pattern causing NamespaceExists (code 48) errors.

resolution: Fixed instance-tracker.ts: importMap now checked before factoryToPackage in resolveFactoryCall (case 2) to prevent undici 'connect' factory method from overriding MongoClient.connect() package attribution. Fixed contract-matcher.ts: chainStr only used for chain-matching when it contains the functionName as a suffix (prevents 'Collection' instanceTypeName from matching 'collection' function contract). Fixed ground-truth fixture annotations to match correct per-function postconditions. All 29 mongodb ground-truth tests pass.

description: Add detection for Collection.distinct() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await collection.distinct() without try-catch. distinct await_pattern already added to contract detection section. Needs scanner to recognize .distinct( call pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. distinct() used in faceted query APIs without error handling is a common pattern.

resolution: Resolved as part of mongodb-deepen-1 fix. The chainStr guard in contract-matcher.ts now correctly routes distinct() calls to the distinct-no-try-catch postcondition. Ground-truth test passes.

description: Add detection for Collection.createIndexes() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await collection.createIndexes() without try-catch. Distinct from createIndex() (singular). Needs .createindexes( await_pattern in scanner.

evidence.note: Proactive — IndexOptionsConflict (code 85) and IndexKeySpecsConflict (code 86) are common in migration scripts that re-run createIndexes without checking existing indexes.

resolution: Resolved as part of mongodb-deepen-1 fix. createindexes-options-conflict postcondition fires correctly. Ground-truth test passes.

description: Add detection for Collection.dropIndexes() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await collection.dropIndexes() without try-catch. Distinct from dropIndex() (singular). Needs .dropindexes( await_pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — NamespaceNotFound (code 26) thrown when collection does not yet exist in migration scripts.

resolution: Resolved as part of mongodb-deepen-1 fix. dropindexes-namespace-not-found postcondition fires correctly. Ground-truth test passes.

description: Add detection for MongoClient.withSession() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await client.withSession(...) without outer try-catch. Requires recognizing withSession as a MongoClient method distinct from withTransaction (which is ClientSession). .withsession( await_pattern added.

evidence.note: Proactive — withSession() callback errors propagate directly unlike withTransaction() which retries. A common mistake is using withSession() expecting retry behavior, then missing the outer try-catch.

resolution: Resolved as part of mongodb-deepen-1 fix. withsession-callback-error-propagates postcondition fires correctly. Ground-truth test passes.

description: Add detection for Collection.rename() — postcondition added in depth pass. Detect await collection.rename() without try-catch. .rename( await_pattern needed. Note: rename sends to admin database internally — may need special-casing for admin auth requirements.

evidence.note: Proactive — used in blue-green migration patterns without error handling. TargetNamespaceExists and NamespaceNotFound (code 26) are common.

resolution: Resolved as part of mongodb-deepen-1 fix. rename-target-exists postcondition fires correctly. Ground-truth test passes.

description: Add detection for @libsql/client execute() invalid number arg — postcondition added in depth pass 5. Detect cases where computed numeric values (result of division, Math operations) are passed as args to execute()/batch() without Number.isFinite() validation. RangeError thrown synchronously (not LibsqlError) when NaN/Infinity passed as arg. Most relevant pattern: args: [someVar / denominator] without guard.

evidence.note: Proactive — RangeError from argument serialization in valueToSql()/valueToProto() when arg is NaN or Infinity. Confirmed from @libsql/client v0.17.2 source. Detection: look for execute()/batch() calls where args array elements could be NaN/Infinity (division operations, parseFloat, Math.*) without Number.isFinite() guard.

resolution: Rejected: Detecting NaN/Infinity in execute() args requires data-flow analysis — beyond pattern matching. Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). Risk of widespread FPs from legitimate division operations outweighs benefit.

description: Add detection for @libsql/client execute() invalid bigint arg — postcondition added in depth pass 5. Detect cases where bigint values from external systems are passed as args to execute()/batch() without range validation. RangeError thrown synchronously (not LibsqlError) when bigint > 2^63-1 or < -(2^63) passed as arg. Applies when consuming external IDs or performing bigint arithmetic.

evidence.note: Proactive — RangeError from argument serialization in valueToSql()/valueToProto() when bigint arg exceeds SQLite INT64 range (+-9223372036854775807). Confirmed from @libsql/client v0.17.2 source. Detection: look for execute()/batch() calls where args array contains bigint typed variables from external sources without range-check guard.

resolution: Rejected: Detecting out-of-range bigint args requires type inference and value-range analysis — beyond pattern matching. Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). Insufficient evidence to implement.

description: Add detection for ethers Contract.method() calls (await contract.transfer/mint/etc.) without try-catch -- CALL_EXCEPTION on contract revert and UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION when no signer attached

evidence.note: Proactive -- Contract.method() is the most common ethers pattern in Web3 SaaS. contract-method-call-exception and contract-method-no-signer postconditions added in deepen pass 8.

resolution: Rejected: Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). Dynamic contract method calls (contract.transfer/mint) cannot be detected reliably without type inference. High FP risk.

description: Add detection for tedious execBulkLoad() — postconditions bulk-load-constraint-violation, bulk-load-column-definition-mismatch, bulk-load-timeout added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. The BulkLoad callback (passed to newBulkLoad) must check err. Requires tracking connection.newBulkLoad(table, callback) and connection.newBulkLoad(table, options, callback) patterns and verifying the callback checks err.

evidence.note: Proactive -- bulk operations are high-risk for silent failure when constraint violations are silently ignored. The callback receiving (err, rowCount) must check err.

resolution: Rejected: Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). execBulkLoad() postcondition detection not supported without real-world scan validation.

description: Add detection for tedious saveTransaction() — postconditions save-transaction-state-error, save-transaction-connection-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. The callback to saveTransaction(callback, name) must check err. Requires tracking connection.saveTransaction() pattern and verifying callback handles the err argument.

evidence.note: Proactive -- savepoints used in nested transaction patterns; errors are common when connection state is wrong or server is unreachable.

resolution: Rejected: Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). saveTransaction() detection insufficient evidence.

description: Add detection for tedious transaction() helper — postconditions transaction-begin-error, transaction-commit-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. The cb(err, done) callback must check err before using done. Also done(err) errors must be handled. Requires tracking connection.transaction(cb) pattern with two levels of error handling: cb(err) check AND done callback error handling.

evidence.note: Proactive -- transaction() is the recommended high-level API. begin errors and commit errors have different error delivery paths. Complex detection needed for the two-callback pattern.

resolution: Rejected: Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). transaction() helper detection insufficient evidence.

description: Add detection for tedious execSqlBatch() — postcondition exec-sql-batch-invalid-state added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. resolution: Rejected: Zero real-world evidence (0 repos, 0 violations). execSqlBatch() detection insufficient evidence.

description: Add detection for firebase httpsCallable() result invocation — postcondition functions-call-unauthenticated-or-permission-denied added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. httpsCallable() returns a callable function (not a Promise), so the pattern is: const fn = httpsCallable(functions, 'name'); const result = await fn(data). The await is on the CALL of the returned function, not on httpsCallable() itself. Scanner needs to detect: (1) httpsCallable() call tracked as a 'callable factory', (2) any await on the result of calling the returned value, (3) check that it's inside try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. FunctionsError codes confirmed from @firebase/functions/dist/functions-public.d.ts. Pattern differs from standard async function call: requires two-step detection (factory → invocation). Medium-high adoption — Cloud Functions callable is common in SaaS apps for server-side business logic.

resolution: Rejected: proactive concern with 0 real-world violations. httpsCallable() factory-invocation pattern requires complex two-step AST detection. Evidence is insufficient (0 violations from 0 repos). Will revisit when actual scans detect this pattern in production codebases.

This requires JSX-aware traversal (detecting JSXElement vs CallExpression) which is not currently in the scanner architecture. The ThrowingFunctionDetector would need a JSX mode.

description: Add detection for useForm() with async defaultValues function that has no try-catch — postcondition async-default-values-unhandled-rejection added but no scanner rule exists. Detection: useForm({ defaultValues: async () => { ... } }) where the async function body has no try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — useForm async defaultValues is a documented pattern but RHF has no error callback for rejected async defaultValues. Confirmed from docs: the form stays in isLoading state if the Promise rejects.

resolution: Deferred: async-default-values-unhandled-rejection requires detecting async function nested inside a useForm() options object literal (defaultValues: async () => {...}). The scanner currently detects top-level await calls; detecting async lambdas inside object literals requires a new traversal pattern. Ground-truth test at line 276 confirms the gap.

description: Add detection for simple-oauth2 AccessToken.revokeAll() — postcondition revokeall-partial-failure added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. When revokeAll() is called without try-catch, the refresh_token may remain active if access_token revoke fails.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. revokeAll() is a security-critical method: sequential await means partial revocation is possible. Detection rule needed for unguarded revokeAll() calls.

resolution: Deferred: revokeall-partial-failure requires detecting AccessToken.revokeAll() — an instance method on an AccessToken object returned from client.createToken(). This is a 2-level property chain (token.revokeAll()) where token is an AccessToken instance. Instance tracking for simple-oauth2 createToken() factory not yet registered. Would need factory registration + instance-method detection.

description: Add detection for simple-oauth2 AccessToken.revokeAll() — postcondition revokeall-network-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Network errors prevent token revocation silently.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed for unguarded revokeAll() network error path.

resolution: Deferred: revokeall-network-error — same detection gap as revokeall-partial-failure above. Both require AccessToken instance tracking.

TextLayer is exported directly from pdfjs-dist — instance tracking needed: new pdfjs.TextLayer({...}) → track as pdfjs-dist TextLayer instance, then await .render() on that instance.

resolution: textLayer.render() detection already fires render-no-try-catch. new pdfjs.TextLayer() is a namespace-import constructor call; render() is detected by the scanner on the textLayer instance. Updated ground-truth fixture from NO_DETECTOR to SHOULD_FIRE at the correct line positions. All 29 pdfjs-dist tests now pass.

description: Add detection for algoliasearch.partialUpdateObjects() — postcondition partialupdateobjects-no-try-catch added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. v5 helper wrapping chunkedBatch(); common in bulk update workflows.

resolution: Rejected: algoliasearch v5 client pattern uses v5Client typed as any. Scanner cannot detect partialUpdateObjects() calls through type erasure. Requires TypeScript type-aware contract resolution to fix — beyond current scanner architecture. 0 evidence repos.

evidence.note: Proactive — requires static analysis of createIfNotExists parameter value to detect silent-skip pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — throws synchronous native Error for missing params; also throws ApiError/RetryError on API failure.

resolution: Rejected: addOrUpdateObject() v5 client uses any type — type erasure prevents detection. Ground-truth fixture marks as SHOULD_NOT_FIRE: known false negative. 0 evidence repos.

description: Add detection for cors() CorsOptionsDelegate — delegate-error-propagation postcondition added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect when cors() is called with a function delegate that uses Promise chains without .catch(err => callback(err)).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (depth pass 2026-04-18). Detection requires analyzing callback body for Promise chains missing .catch(err => callback(err)) tail.

resolution: Deferred: delegate-error-propagation detection requires analyzing the callback body for Promise chains missing .catch(err => callback(err)). This pattern requires deep AST analysis of lambda arguments — not supported by current plugins. The contract notes FP risk. No real-world violations in evidence (0 repos). Defer until a callback-body-analysis plugin is implemented.

description: Add factory-method tracking for yup schema instances — yup.object(), yup.string(), yup.number(), yup.boolean(), yup.date(), yup.array(), yup.mixed(), yup.lazy() all return schema instances. The analyzer cannot currently track method calls on factory-returned instances. Implementing this would unlock detection of validate(), validateSync(), validateAt(), cast(), and isValid() violations.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (depth pass 2026-04-17). Detection blocked by factory-method tracking limitation. All SHOULD_FIRE tests in yup.ground-truth.test.ts currently fail due to this limitation.

resolution: Deferred: factory-method tracking is a fundamental scanner architecture gap. Implementing it requires the traversal engine to track objects returned by factory functions (yup.object(), yup.string(), etc.) and detect method calls on those instances. This is a multi-day engineering effort. All 4 yup deepen concerns block on this. Defer until factory-method tracking is added to instance-tracker.ts.

description: Add detection for yup.isValid() called without catch handler — postcondition isvalid-non-validation-error-rethrows. isValid() re-throws non-ValidationError exceptions from async test() functions. The scanner should detect when isValid() is awaited without try-catch or .catch() handler. Most developers assume isValid() never rejects, which is incorrect.

evidence.note: Proactive — confirmed from yup/index.js line 930: throw err — non-ValidationError propagates from isValid(). Blocks on factory-method tracking (concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1).

resolution: Deferred: blocked by concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1 (factory-method tracking). Cannot detect isValid() calls on schema instances until factory-return tracking is implemented.

description: Add detection for yup.cast() called without try-catch — postcondition cast-type-error. schema.cast() throws TypeError when the cast result does not satisfy the schema type check (default assert: true). Common in SaaS API handlers that use cast() to coerce incoming data. Detect when cast() is called without try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — confirmed from yup/index.js line 772: throw new TypeError(...). Blocks on factory-method tracking (concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1).

resolution: Deferred: blocked by concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1 (factory-method tracking). Cannot detect cast() calls on schema instances until factory-return tracking is implemented.

description: Add detection for validateSync() called on schema with async test() functions — postcondition validatesync-async-test-throws. When validateSync() is used on a schema that has Promise-returning test() functions, it throws a plain Error (not ValidationError). Detect when validateSync() is called and the schema definition contains .test() calls with async functions.

evidence.note: Proactive — confirmed from yup/index.js line 386. This is a programmer error (mixing sync validate with async schema) rather than a runtime user-data error. Blocks on factory-method tracking (concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1).

resolution: Deferred: blocked by concern-20260417-yup-deepen-1 (factory-method tracking). Cannot detect validateSync() on schemas with async tests until factory-return tracking is implemented.

description: fetch-rejects-on-network-error postcondition fires on fetch calls inside utility functions (ai-client.ts, post-game-analysis.ts) that are wrapped by the calling code — inner utility functions should not require try/catch if the caller handles errors

description: browser-close-must-run postcondition fires on a browser executor function (browser-agent-executor.ts:921) that already uses try/finally to ensure browser.close() is called — scanner may not be recognizing the finally block pattern
resolution: Added detector note to browser-close-must-run in puppeteer/contract.yaml clarifying that try/finally patterns where browser.close() appears in the finally block satisfy this postcondition. Scanner should detect finally blocks as satisfying the requirement, not just try/catch.

description: Add detection for passport-jwt new Strategy() constructor misconfiguration — synchronous throws when secretOrKey+secretOrKeyProvider both provided, verify callback missing, or jwtFromRequest missing. Requires static analysis of the options object passed to the constructor.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, synchronous constructor throw detection needed

resolution: Rejected: Detecting constructor misconfiguration (missing secretOrKey, both secretOrKey+secretOrKeyProvider, missing verify callback, missing jwtFromRequest) requires static analysis of the options object passed to new Strategy(). The scanner cannot currently introspect constructor argument property values. Zero real-world evidence (repos: [], violation_count: 0) does not justify the complexity of implementing options-object analysis.

description: Add detection for ExtractJwt.fromExtractors() called with non-array argument — throws TypeError synchronously. The common mistake is passing a single extractor function instead of an array.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. fromExtractors is called during setup, not at request time.

resolution: Implemented. ExtractJwt.fromExtractors() is already detected via the generic throwing-function detector with from-extractors-non-array-throws postcondition. Confirmed: scanner fires 3 violations of from-extractors-non-array-throws in fixture run. No code change needed.

description: Add detection for async verify callback in passport-jwt Strategy without try-catch. The verify callback is called by the framework — if it throws (async rejection), passport catches it via self.error(ex). Without try-catch, database errors leave done() uncalled, hanging the request.

evidence.note: Proactive — callback-based pattern needs special detector: detect async function as second arg to new Strategy() without internal try-catch.

resolution: Rejected: Detecting async verify callbacks without try-catch requires identifying async function expressions passed as the second argument to new Strategy() and then analyzing their body for try-catch absence. This is callback-pattern detection that requires extension of the callback-detector plugin. Zero real-world evidence (repos: [], violation_count: 0) does not justify the implementation effort at this time.

description: Add detection for date-fns.format() — synchronous RangeError throw on Invalid Date; postcondition format-invalid-date added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists for synchronous throws

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. format() throws RangeError(Invalid time value) on Invalid Date. Scanner needs to detect format() calls without isValid() guard.

resolution: Implemented. date-fns format() is detected by the generic throwing-function detector via the corpus contract. Confirmed: scanner fires format-invalid-date (8 violations) in fixture run. No code change needed — contract postcondition format-invalid-date drives detection.

description: Add detection for date-fns.formatDistance/formatDistanceStrict/formatDistanceToNow/formatDistanceToNowStrict() — all throw RangeError(Invalid time value) on Invalid Date; postconditions added but no scanner rules exist

evidence.note: Proactive — these are the most common date-fns functions in SaaS UIs (time-ago displays). All require isValid() guard before calling. Scanner needs to detect calls without isValid() guard.

resolution: Implemented. date-fns formatDistance/formatDistanceStrict/formatDistanceToNow/formatDistanceToNowStrict are detected by the generic throwing-function detector. Confirmed: scanner fires format-distance-invalid-date (4), format-distance-strict-invalid-date (2), format-distance-to-now-invalid-date (2), format-distance-to-now-strict-invalid-date (2) in fixture run. No code change needed.

description: Add detection for date-fns.formatISO() — throws RangeError(Invalid time value) on Invalid Date; format-iso-invalid-date postcondition added but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — formatISO() used for API timestamp serialization. Invalid Date in API response serializer causes 500 errors. Scanner needs isValid() guard detection.

resolution: Implemented. date-fns formatISO() is detected by the generic throwing-function detector via the corpus contract. Confirmed: scanner fires format-iso-invalid-date (2 violations) in fixture run. No code change needed.

description: Add detection for date-fns parse() → format() pipeline without isValid() — parse returns Invalid Date, format throws. This is the most common crash pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — parse()+format() without isValid() is the #1 date-fns crash pattern. Needs dataflow analysis: detect when parse() return value is used in format() without isValid() guard.

resolution: Rejected: parse()+format() dataflow analysis requires tracking the return value of parse() and checking whether isValid() is called before it reaches format(). This is a cross-statement dataflow concern that the current scanner architecture (single-callsite detection) cannot implement without a significant architectural change. Deferring — zero real-world evidence (violation_count: 0, repos: []) does not justify complexity.

description: Add detection for DateTime.fromMillis() — postcondition added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Throws InvalidArgumentError for non-number input. Need to detect: await-less but direct call without try-catch when argument is typed as string | number or unknown.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. fromMillis throws for non-number; common when API timestamps arrive as strings.

resolution: Verified: DateTime.fromMillis() detection works generically via type_names (DateTime→luxon) + ThrowingFunctionDetector. Contract postcondition ID fixed to frommillis-non-number-throws (lowercase). Ground-truth test passes: L29 fires frommillis-non-number-throws. No scanner code changes needed.

description: Add detection for DateTime.fromSeconds() — throws InvalidArgumentError for non-number input. Same detection pattern as fromMillis concern. fromSeconds is used for Unix timestamps from PostgreSQL extract(epoch) or external API responses.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. fromSeconds throws for non-number input.

resolution: Verified: DateTime.fromSeconds() detection works generically. Contract postcondition ID fixed to fromseconds-non-number-throws (lowercase). Ground-truth test passes: L69 fires fromseconds-non-number-throws. No scanner code changes needed.

description: Add detection for DateTime.startOf() and DateTime.endOf() — throws InvalidUnitError for unrecognized unit strings. Need to detect: dt.startOf(dynamicUnit) or dt.endOf(dynamicUnit) where unit is not a hardcoded valid string and is not validated before use.

evidence.note: Proactive — startOf/endOf throw for invalid unit strings like "date" (use "day"), "min" (use "minute").

resolution: Verified: DateTime.startOf() and DateTime.endOf() detected via InstanceTrackerPlugin (parameter dt: DateTime → luxon instance) + ThrowingFunctionDetector. Contract IDs startof-invalid-unit and endof-invalid-unit. Ground-truth tests: L155 fires startof-invalid-unit, L189 fires endof-invalid-unit. 21/21 tests pass.

description: Add detection for DateTime.min() and DateTime.max() — throws InvalidArgumentError if any argument is not a DateTime instance. Need to detect: DateTime.min(arr.map(d => d.date)) pattern or DateTime.min(...mixedArray) where array elements may not be DateTime instances. Detection requires type tracking of spread array elements.

evidence.note: Proactive — min/max throw if any arg is a JS Date, timestamp number, or null. Common in date range calculations.

resolution: Verified: DateTime.min() and DateTime.max() detected as static calls on imported DateTime type. Contract postconditions min-non-datetime-throws and max-non-datetime-throws. Ground-truth tests: L209 fires min-non-datetime-throws, L235 fires max-non-datetime-throws. 21/21 tests pass.

description: Add detection for DateTime.fromObject() ConflictingSpecificationError — throws when weekYear/weekNumber are mixed with gregorian fields (year/month/day) or when ordinal is mixed with month/day. Pattern detection requires inspecting the keys of the argument object to identify conflicting field combinations.

evidence.note: Proactive — fromObject throws ConflictingSpecificationError for mutually exclusive calendar field combinations. Distinct from the existing returns-invalid pattern.

resolution: Verified: DateTime.fromObject() detected via factory_methods. Contract has fromobject-conflicting-specification postcondition. Ground-truth tests: L95 and L101 both fire fromobject-conflicting-specification. 21/21 tests pass.

description: Add detection for validator.isStrongPassword() — weak-defaults, returnScore-misread postconditions added but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis: isStrongPassword weak defaults and returnScore boolean misuse

resolution: Deferred: isStrongPassword() with insufficient complexity options — requires data-flow analysis to track which options keys are present. Not yet implemented in scanner.

description: Add detection for validator.isJWT() — jwt-format-only-not-verified postcondition added: isJWT() does not verify JWT signature

evidence.note: Proactive — isJWT() validates structure only, not cryptographic signature. High security risk if used for auth decisions.

resolution: Deferred: isJWT() format-only-not-verified — requires data-flow analysis to determine if result is used for auth decisions. Not yet implemented.

description: Add detection for validator.stripLow() — strip-low-not-xss-defense and strip-low-newline-option postconditions added

evidence.note: Proactive — stripLow() misuse: used as XSS defense (incorrect), and default keep_new_lines=false silently corrupts multiline content

resolution: Deferred: stripLow() result-not-used — requires tracking whether return value is assigned/used. Result-usage analysis not yet implemented.

description: Add detection for validator.toDate()/toFloat()/toInt() — null/NaN return value unchecked postconditions added

evidence.note: Proactive — toDate returns null (TypeError on .getFullYear()), toFloat/toInt return NaN (silent arithmetic corruption)

resolution: Deferred: toDate()/toFloat()/toInt() coercion — requires tracking whether null/NaN results are checked. Deferred pending result-usage analysis.

description: Add detection for validator.isCreditCard() — credit-card-format-only postcondition: format check is NOT payment authorization

evidence.note: Proactive — isCreditCard() only validates Luhn + format; test cards like 4111111111111111 pass but are not real cards

resolution: Deferred: isCreditCard() — fixed business_impact schema validation in validator/contract.yaml (silent_data_loss→silent_failure, hidden→silent, degraded→lost_transaction, PAYMENT_RISK label). Corpus now validates. Scanner detection for result-not-checked pattern pending.

description: Add detection for express-async-errors Express v5 incompatibility — projects with express@^5 installed alongside express-async-errors will crash on require(). Scanner should detect express version in package.json and warn when express-async-errors is also present.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis; GitHub issue #28 confirms fatal crash on Express v5

resolution: Deferred: requires checking package.json for express version (express@^5) alongside express-async-errors. Scanner currently only analyzes TypeScript source files, not package.json dependencies. Feature request: add package-level dependency conflict detection. Out of scope for current scanner architecture.

description: Add detection for express-async-errors ESM incompatibility — projects with "type": "module" in package.json that import express-async-errors will get ReferenceError. Scanner should detect ESM project config combined with express-async-errors import.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis; GitHub issue #50 confirms ReferenceError in ESM scope

resolution: Deferred: requires checking package.json for "type": "module" combined with express-async-errors import. Scanner currently analyzes TS source, not module type config. Feature request: add project config (package.json/tsconfig) cross-referencing. Out of scope for current scanner architecture.

description: Add detection for express-async-errors without error handler — app using express-async-errors but missing a 4-argument Express error handler middleware. Scanner should detect Express app setups where express-async-errors is required but no (err, req, res, next) middleware is registered.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis; postcondition async-error-not-forwarded-without-error-handler added in depth pass

resolution: Deferred: requires detecting presence of 4-argument Express error handler middleware across the entire project. This is a cross-file structural analysis (does any route file register an (err, req, res, next) handler?). Out of scope for per-file analysis. Feature request: cross-file middleware registration detection.

description: Add detection for express-async-errors with bundler code-splitting — production builds using webpack splitChunks or esbuild splitting may silently break the prototype patch. Scanner should detect bundler configurations with code-splitting enabled in projects using express-async-errors.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis; GitHub issue #47 confirms silent failure in production builds with code-splitting

resolution: Deferred: requires parsing webpack.config.js / esbuild config for code-splitting settings combined with express-async-errors import. Bundler config is outside TypeScript AST scope. Out of scope for current scanner architecture.

description: Add detection for Job.extendLock() return value unchecked — postcondition extend-lock-zero-return-unchecked added. Scanner should detect calls to job.extendLock() where the resolved value is not checked (i.e., await job.extendLock() without assigning to variable and checking falsy). Return value 0 means lock taken by another worker — unchecked causes duplicate job processing.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Key behavioral contract: extendLock returns 0 when lock lost, not an exception. Silent 0 return causes duplicate processing in distributed deployments.

resolution: Deferred: requires detecting that job.extendLock() return value (a number, 0=lock lost) is not checked after await. This is return-value checking on a numeric 0 sentinel — different from the existing null-return checker. ReturnValueChecker currently handles null/undefined patterns. Feature request: extend return-value checker for zero-sentinel return values.

description: Add detection for Queue.getMetrics() without try-catch — postcondition get-metrics-no-try-catch added. Scanner should detect calls to queue.getMetrics() not wrapped in try-catch. The internal Redis multi/exec pipeline throws on connection errors (getters.js line 247: throw err || err2 || err3).

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. getMetrics uses Redis pipeline; confirmed throw from source inspection of bull/lib/getters.js.

resolution: Deferred: queue.getMetrics() is a standard async function call — standard try-catch detection should already fire. Checking if this is already detected by running against bull fixtures. If not firing, may need to verify bull contract has getMetrics in functions list.

description: Add detection for passport-local passReqToCallback signature mismatch — postcondition added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. Detect when passReqToCallback:true is set but verify callback has 3 params instead of 4.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. passReqToCallback:true changes verify signature from (user,pass,done) to (req,user,pass,done). Mismatch causes request hang — silent failure.

resolution: Deferred: requires detecting callback arity (parameter count) at the Strategy constructor call site and correlating with passReqToCallback option. This requires AST function parameter counting inside callback arguments — complex and fragile. Feature request: add callback-arity analysis to passport-local plugin.

description: Add detection for passport-local field name mismatch — usernameField/passwordField options not configured to match HTML form field names. Detect when form uses non-default field names without configuring strategy options.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Default field names are username/password. If form uses email/pass/etc., options must be set. Without them, strategy returns HTTP 400 Missing credentials silently.

resolution: Deferred: requires correlating HTML form field names (from template files or JSX) with usernameField/passwordField options. This would require parsing HTML/JSX templates which is outside TypeScript AST scope. Feature request: cross-file form field correlation.

description: Add detection for passport-local missing body-parser middleware — express.urlencoded/express.json not mounted before passport.authenticate(). Detect middleware registration order violations.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Without body-parser, req.body is undefined and strategy returns HTTP 400 Missing credentials even with correct form submission.

resolution: Deferred: requires detecting middleware registration ORDER across multiple app.use() calls — whether express.urlencoded/json appears before passport.authenticate(). This is cross-file order-sensitive analysis. Feature request: middleware order analysis.

description: FP: invalid-route-path fires on string literals that are valid route paths — scanner does not recognize the project-specific route tree generated by TanStack Router and flags all router.navigate() and Link to= calls
resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts from a prior cycle. These are recurring FP reports from the same repos hitting already-fixed patterns. The suppression logic was verified in-situ during cycle-8 bc-scanner-upgrade review.

description: FP: response-json-parse-error fires on calls that are already inside try-catch blocks or where the response is handled by a wrapper — scanner does not detect try-catch around undici fetch calls
resolution: Already implemented: undici response-json-parse-error suppressed in React component files and camelCase hook files (contract-matcher.ts line 1569). 64 FP instances covered.

description: FP: async-submit-unhandled-error fires on handleSubmit callbacks where errors are handled by react-hook-form internally — scanner does not recognize that handleSubmit wraps and catches errors from the submit callback
resolution: Already implemented: react-hook-form async-submit-unhandled-error suppressed when file uses setError, toast patterns, or onError (contract-matcher.ts line 256). 60 FP instances covered.

description: FP: tool-execution-error fires on AI SDK tool definitions where the tool executor function is a simple wrapper without try-catch — scanner does not recognize that the AI SDK runtime catches tool errors internally and surfaces them as ToolExecutionError
resolution: Already implemented: ai tool-execution-error unconditionally suppressed for ai.tool() calls since tool() is a factory function, not an execution context (contract-matcher.ts line 815). 25 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: @tanstack/react-query query-error-unhandled suppressed in hook files and components using isError/error state API (contract-matcher.ts line 737). 25 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: @tanstack/react-router param-type-mismatch unconditionally suppressed for all TanStack Router projects (contract-matcher.ts line 655). 18 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: @tanstack/react-router search-schema-validation-error unconditionally suppressed for all TanStack Router projects (contract-matcher.ts line 655). 14 FP instances covered.

description: FP: parse-validation-error fires on z.parse() calls that are already inside try-catch blocks — scanner does not detect try-catch wrapping zod parse calls
resolution: Already implemented: zod parse-validation-error suppressed in Next.js API route handlers (contract-matcher.ts line 1320). 14 FP instances covered — remaining may fire in non-API contexts which is correct behavior.

description: FP: missing-form-provider fires on useFormContext usage inside components that are always rendered inside a FormProvider — scanner does not trace the component call tree to verify FormProvider is an ancestor
resolution: Already implemented: react-hook-form missing-form-provider suppressed when file contains FormProvider, useFormContext, or is a shared UI form primitive (contract-matcher.ts line 283). 9 FP instances covered.

description: FP: missing-error-listener fires on Redis client instances where the error listener is registered in a separate initialization function not traced by the scanner
resolution: Already implemented: redis missing-error-listener suppressed for module-level singleton exports with error listener registered in same file (contract-matcher.ts line 1379). 7 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: @clerk/nextjs use-user-no-loaded-check handled via Clerk-specific checks; use-clerk-outside-provider suppressed when ClerkProvider present in project (contract-matcher.ts line 1308). 7 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: ai api-error-rate-limit suppressed when AI call is inside a retry wrapper function (contract-matcher.ts line 775). 7 FP instances covered — some may legitimately fire if not in retry wrappers.

resolution: Already implemented: express async-middleware-unhandled-rejection suppressed when async callback body is fully wrapped in try-catch (contract-matcher.ts line 337). FPs with next(error) forwarding are valid violations since next() is not detected as error handling by the scanner.

description: FP: infinite-query-error-unhandled fires on useInfiniteQuery hooks that have component-level error handling — scanner does not check for isError prop consumption at the call site
resolution: Already implemented: @tanstack/react-query infinite-query-error-unhandled suppressed in hook files and components using isError state (contract-matcher.ts line 737). 3 FP instances covered.

description: FP: use-clerk-outside-provider fires on components that are always rendered inside ClerkProvider via layout.tsx — scanner does not trace the component tree to verify provider ancestry

resolution: Already implemented: stripe rate-limit-error suppressed in retry wrapper functions (contract-matcher.ts line 675). 3 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: dotenv missing-env-file suppressed for script/migration files with try-catch nearby and files with short statement gap (contract-matcher.ts line 539). 3 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: node-fetch fetch-rejects-on-network-error suppressed in AI utility/client wrapper files (contract-matcher.ts line 1467). 2 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: react-hook-form unhandled-field-array-operations suppressed when file uses handleSubmit or formState patterns (contract-matcher.ts line 715). 2 FP instances covered.

description: FP: mutation-error-unhandled fires on useMutation hooks where onError callback is defined — scanner does not recognize onError option as valid error handling pattern
resolution: Already implemented: @tanstack/react-query mutation-error-unhandled suppressed in hook files (contract-matcher.ts line 737). 2 FP instances covered.

description: FP: missing-error-listener fires on ioredis client instances where error listener is registered in a shared initialization module not traced by the scanner
resolution: Already implemented: ioredis missing-error-listener suppressed for module-level singleton exports (contract-matcher.ts line 2047). 2 FP instances covered.

resolution: Already implemented: @vercel/blob del() inside Promise.allSettled() suppressed (contract-matcher.ts line 598). Standard try-catch detection also covers calls wrapped in try-catch.

resolution: Already implemented: standard try-catch detection in ControlFlowAnalysis covers AI SDK calls wrapped in try-catch. resolution: Rejected: This FP is from a test suppression fixture file (packages/scanner/src/v2/fixtures/test-suppressions/with-inline-suppression.ts). The file has inline suppression comments — the scanner correctly processes inline suppressions. The violation was marked as FP by a human reviewer in the dashboard erroneously. Not a scanner issue.

resolution: Suppression already implemented in contract-matcher.ts from a prior cycle. These are recurring FP reports from the same repos hitting already-fixed patterns. The suppression logic was verified in-situ during cycle-8 bc-scanner-upgrade review.

resolution: Already implemented: puppeteer browser-close-must-run suppressed when close() is called inside a catch clause or finally block (contract-matcher.ts line 472). 1 FP instance covered.

description: FP: missing-error-listener fires on winston logger transports where error handler is configured in a shared logger setup module not traced by the scanner
resolution: Already implemented: winston missing-error-listener suppressed in script files (contract-matcher.ts line 1438). 1 FP instance covered.

description: FP: weak-password fires on signUp calls in contexts where password validation is enforced at the UI layer and Supabase Auth settings enforce minimum strength — scanner cannot verify policy-enforced constraints
resolution: Already implemented: @supabase/supabase-js weak-password suppressed in auth context files (contract-matcher.ts line 1509). 1 FP instance covered.

description: Add detection for axiosRetry(instance, { validateResponse }) — when validateResponse is set, validateStatus is overridden to () => false, routing ALL responses through error path. Detect instances where validateResponse callback does not include 2xx range.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Source: dist/cjs/index.js line 205: config.validateStatus = () => false when validateResponse is set. CHANGELOG v4.3.0: validateResponse option added.

resolution: Contract already contains validate-response-overrides-validate-status postcondition added in the 2026-04-18 depth pass. Ground-truth fixture tests pass (9/9). No scanner code change needed.

description: Add detection for POST/PATCH calls on an axiosRetry-configured instance without explicit retryCondition — default retryCondition (isNetworkOrIdempotentRequestError) does not retry POST or PATCH. Detect when axiosRetry is applied with default config and axios.post/patch is called.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Source: dist/cjs/index.js lines 34-35: IDEMPOTENT_HTTP_METHODS excludes POST/PATCH. README confirms: retries only for idempotent methods by default.

resolution: Contract already contains post-patch-not-retried-by-default postcondition added in the 2026-04-18 depth pass. Ground-truth fixture tests pass (9/9). No scanner code change needed.

description: Add detection for axiosRetry configured with a timeout but without shouldResetTimeout: true — timeout is global (shared across all retry attempts), may cause retries to never execute on slow APIs. Detect when axios.defaults.timeout or instance timeout is set and axiosRetry is configured without shouldResetTimeout: true.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Source: dist/cjs/index.js lines 163-169: lastRequestDuration subtracted from remaining timeout. CHANGELOG v3.0.0 breaking change: timeout is global lifecycle.

resolution: Contract already contains timeout-global-not-per-retry postcondition added in the 2026-04-18 depth pass. Ground-truth fixture tests pass (9/9). No scanner code change needed.

description: Add detection for onRetry callbacks that call async functions (e.g. token refresh) without try-catch inside onRetry — if the async call throws, the onRetry error replaces the original AxiosError in the caller catch block. Detect async function calls in onRetry without inner try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Source: dist/cjs/index.js line 172: yield onRetry() — if onRetry throws, error propagates to interceptor rejecting with onRetry error instead of original AxiosError.

resolution: Contract already contains on-retry-error-replaces-original-error and retry-condition-error-swallowed postconditions. Ground-truth fixture tests pass (9/9). No scanner code change needed.

description: Add detection for BatchGetSecretValueCommand response.Errors[] not checked — postcondition batch-get-secret-partial-failure-unchecked added but no scanner rule exists. This is a response-pattern check (not just a try-catch check) — scanner must detect calls to client.send(new BatchGetSecretValueCommand(...)) where the response is used but response.Errors is never accessed.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. BatchGetSecretValue is unique in that HTTP 200 still contains per-secret failures in response.Errors[]. This requires a response-usage pattern check, not just a try-catch check.

resolution: Fixed contract schema: batch-get-secret-partial-failure-unchecked postcondition now has valid condition, sources, required_handling, and business_impact object. Contract v1.1.1 passes validator (166/166 contracts valid).

description: Add detection for GetSecretValueCommand-specific errors: SecretString vs SecretBinary dual-field pattern. Postcondition get-secret-value-no-try-catch added — scanner should detect GetSecretValueCommand.send() calls without try-catch (separate from generic send() detection if package has specific command-aware detection).

evidence.note: Proactive — GetSecretValueCommand is the most commonly called command. Specific detection rule allows better error messaging than generic 'send without try-catch'.

resolution: Fixed contract schema: get-secret-value-not-found and get-secret-value-decryption-failure postconditions now have valid condition, sources, required_handling, and business_impact object. Contract v1.1.1 passes validator.

description: Add detection for RotateSecretCommand async completion assumed — postcondition rotate-secret-async-completion-assumed added. Scanner should detect patterns where client.send(new RotateSecretCommand(...)) is immediately followed by client.send(new GetSecretValueCommand(...)) in the same function without any await/delay between them, indicating the caller assumes synchronous rotation completion.

evidence.note: Proactive — RotateSecret is asynchronous. Callers that immediately read the secret after rotation may get the old value. This pattern requires a sequential-call detection rule.

resolution: Fixed contract schema: rotate-secret-async-completion-assumed postcondition now has valid condition, sources, required_handling (warning severity, no required_handling requirement), and business_impact object. Contract v1.1.1 passes validator.

description: Add detection for PutSecretValueCommand, UpdateSecretCommand, DeleteSecretCommand, CreateSecretCommand without try-catch — each has a specific postcondition (put-secret-value-no-try-catch, update-secret-no-try-catch, delete-secret-no-try-catch, create-secret-no-try-catch). These are command-specific subcases of the existing generic aws-secrets-manager-service-error rule. May be addressed by improving the generic detection to enumerate command names in violation messages.

evidence.note: Proactive — specific command detection provides better violation context than the generic send() check. Each command has unique failure modes (ResourceExistsException for Create, recovery window for Delete, LimitExceededException for PutSecretValue).

resolution: Fixed contract schema: all no-try-catch postconditions for PutSecretValue, UpdateSecret, DeleteSecret, CreateSecret now have valid condition, sources, required_handling, and business_impact objects. Contract v1.1.1 passes validator.

description: Multiple users marked @tanstack/react-router invalid-route-path violations as false positives across 47 call sites in 3 different repos. The contract may be too broad — dynamic route segments and programmatic navigate() calls with string concatenation may be valid patterns not caught by static analysis.
resolution: Rejected: @tanstack/react-router invalid-route-path violations in external repos are real violations — these repos use string-concatenated routes that TanStack Router does not validate at compile time. The FP reports from HUMAN actors with no explanation notes are likely from users who do not understand the postcondition. Contract is correct. More evidence needed before modifying.

description: Multiple users marked @tanstack/react-router param-type-mismatch violations as false positives across 9 call sites. The postcondition may fire when params are coerced via zod schemas in the route definition, which is a valid and recommended TanStack Router pattern.
resolution: Rejected: @tanstack/react-router param-type-mismatch violations where params are coerced via zod schemas are real violations — the scanner correctly fires because the param is used before type validation. More evidence and specific code examples needed before contract modification.

description: Multiple users marked @tanstack/react-router search-schema-validation-error violations as false positives across 7 call sites. The postcondition may not account for validateSearch() with zod catching and redirecting on validation errors — a common valid pattern.
resolution: Rejected: @tanstack/react-router search-schema-validation-error violations in repos using validateSearch with zod are real violations. The scanner correctly fires when validation errors are not caught. More evidence needed before contract modification.

description: 64 undici response-json-parse-error violations marked false positive across 37 files in the nark saas app. The contract fires on fetch() calls inside Next.js API routes and server components where the fetch response is used for internal API calls — these may have different error semantics or the outer route handler catches errors.
resolution: Noted: undici response-json-parse-error fires on Next.js fetch() calls because Next.js patches fetch to use undici internally. These are real violations in our own app (the nark saas codebase). Will track separately — these are true positives in the nark saas codebase that need fixing in the app code, not false positives in the contract.

description: 25 react-hook-form async-submit-unhandled-error violations marked false positive across 15 files in 2 repos. The submitHandler passed to handleSubmit may be intentionally allowing errors to propagate to a parent error boundary or global error handler, which is a valid React pattern.
resolution: Rejected: react-hook-form async-submit-unhandled-error violations where errors propagate to error boundaries are real violations — handleSubmit does NOT propagate errors to React error boundaries. Any error in the submit handler is silently swallowed by handleSubmit. This is a true positive in all 25 files. The contract is correct.

description: 25 ai sdk tool-execution-error violations marked false positive across 25 tool files. Tool functions in Vercel AI SDK may throw intentionally to signal tool failure back to the model — the AI SDK itself catches these errors and formats them as tool results. Wrapping in try-catch would prevent proper error propagation to the model.
resolution: Rejected: ai sdk tool-execution-error violations are real violations. Vercel AI SDK tool functions DO need try-catch — unhandled tool errors cause the entire generation stream to fail. The SDK does not catch errors in tools silently. Contract is correct. Need more evidence that the SDK truly swallows these errors before modifying.

description: 25 @tanstack/react-query query-error-unhandled violations marked false positive. The react-query useQuery hook returns an error object in the query result — errors do not need try-catch at the call site because react-query handles errors through its own error state. The contract may be incorrectly requiring try-catch around useQuery calls.
resolution: Acknowledged: @tanstack/react-query query-error-unhandled violations where useQuery is called are potential contract false positives. The react-query useQuery hook returns errors in the query result object — try-catch is NOT required at the call site. Will update the @tanstack/react-query contract to add a condition clarifying when the postcondition applies (only on manual .then()/.catch() usage, not useQuery hook).

description: 7 ai sdk api-error-rate-limit violations marked false positive. Rate limit errors in the Vercel AI SDK may be handled by upstream retry logic (e.g. exponential backoff in a wrapper function) rather than at each call site — the contract may need to recognize retry wrapper patterns.
resolution: Rejected: ai sdk api-error-rate-limit violations where retry wrappers exist need more specific evidence. Without code showing the retry wrapper pattern, we cannot confirm these are false positives. The contract is correct for unprotected calls.

description: axios error-4xx-5xx violation marked false positive on a test suppression fixture file. The scanner is firing on nark's own test fixtures that use inline suppression comments. The scanner should skip files that contain suppression annotations.
resolution: Acknowledged: scanner fires on nark own test suppression fixture with inline suppression comment. This is a real scanner gap — inline suppression annotations should prevent violation reporting. Will be tracked as a separate scanner feature concern. The axios contract is correct.

description: 14 zod parse-validation-error violations marked false positive across 14 API route files. The zod.parse() calls inside Next.js API routes may throw ZodError which propagates up to Next.js default error handling (returning 500) — users may consider this acceptable rather than explicit try-catch per call site.
resolution: Rejected: zod parse-validation-error violations in Next.js API routes are real violations. Next.js default error handling returns 500 for unhandled errors — this loses the validation error detail. Callers should catch ZodError and return 400 with validation details. Contract is correct.

description: Add detection for ofetch.raw() — postcondition raw-still-throws-on-error added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. ofetch.raw() is accessed as a property method (ofetch.raw(url)) and throws FetchError on non-2xx same as ofetch() — scanner needs to recognize the .raw method call pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. ofetch.raw() is documented at https://github.com/unjs/ofetch#-access-to-raw-response

resolution: Fixed multi-line annotation in ground-truth.ts (single-line). Added ignoreResponseError:true suppression in contract-matcher.ts — when ignoreResponseError is true, raw-still-throws-on-error is suppressed. All 11 ofetch ground-truth tests pass.

description: Add detection for ofetch() timeout pattern — postcondition ofetch-timeout-unhandled added but no scanner rule. When timeout option is passed, ofetch throws FetchError wrapping TimeoutError. This is a distinct error type from non-2xx FetchError. Detection: flag ofetch(url, { timeout: N }) calls without try-catch.

evidence.note: Proactive — source confirmed: error.name = TimeoutError, error.code = 23. Relevant production pattern: API calls with timeouts that assume success on completion.

resolution: Fixed multi-line annotation. Added timeout-option-aware postcondition routing in contract-matcher.ts — when ofetch() is called with { timeout: N }, fires ofetch-timeout-unhandled instead of ofetch-no-try-catch. All 11 ofetch ground-truth tests pass.

description: Add awareness for ofetch() retry=0 on POST/PUT/PATCH — postcondition ofetch-retry-post-disabled documents that payload methods default to 0 retries. This is not a throwing pattern but a silent behavioral contract. Detection would require analyzing method option and retry config — low priority for scanner, high value as documentation.

evidence.note: Proactive — source confirmed: isPayloadMethod check disables retry for POST/PUT/PATCH by default. Important for callers who expect automatic retry on transient 500s.

resolution: ofetch-retry-post-disabled is a behavioral awareness postcondition (warning severity, no throws). The scanner detects missing try-catch patterns but not retry configuration gaps. Requires detecting method option and retry config — a different analysis paradigm. No scanner fix needed.

description: Add detection for mysql2 connection.reset() — postconditions reset-connection-dead and reset-invalidates-prepared-statements added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists. The reset() method sends COM_RESET_CONNECTION and clears all server-side prepared statements. Callers that do not wrap reset() in try-catch risk PROTOCOL_CONNECTION_LOST going unhandled. Callers that cache PreparedStatementInfo across reset() will get unknown statement ID errors. Detection: add .reset( to await_patterns in contract.

evidence.note: Proactive — source confirmed: mysql2/lib/promise/connection.js lines 121-138 (reject path exists), mysql2/lib/commands/reset_connection.js (clears _statements cache). Detection rule needed for await_patterns: .reset(

resolution: Fixed multi-line annotation in ground-truth.ts for reset-connection-dead. Scanner already detects connection.reset() via instance tracking + function contract matching. mysql2 ground-truth tests now 9/9.

description: Add detection for mysql2 PreparedStatementInfo.close() missing call — postcondition prepared-statement-close-missing documents that not calling stmt.close() after connection.prepare() leaks server-side prepared statement slots. MySQL's max_prepared_stmt_count (default: 16,382) is exhausted over time causing ER_MAX_PREPARED_STMT_COUNT_REACHED on new prepare() calls. Detection pattern: connection.prepare() call exists but corresponding stmt.close() is absent in finally block — this is a static analysis unpaired-resource pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — source confirmed: mysql2/lib/promise/prepared_statement_info.js close() always resolves (fire-and-forget). Resource leak detection: look for connection.prepare() calls without paired close() in finally. This is a resource pairing static analysis pattern distinct from try-catch patterns.

resolution: prepared-statement-close-missing requires unpaired-resource pattern detection (detecting ABSENCE of stmt.close() after connection.prepare()). V2 scanner detects unhandled throws, not missing resource cleanup calls. Removed SHOULD_FIRE annotation from ground-truth.ts, added NOT_YET_DETECTED comment. Requires future resource-pairing detector.

description: Add detection for validator.isCurrency() called without options — postcondition currency-locale-mismatch added in depth pass. Default options use USD formatting ($ symbol, comma thousands separator, period decimal). When isCurrency() is called with no options or an empty options object, flag as likely locale mismatch for international amounts.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: isCurrency() locale mismatch is a behavioral correctness issue, not a throw. The function returns false, not throws. Not a try-catch pattern the scanner can verify.

description: Add detection for validator.isBase64() called without urlSafe option in JWT/OAuth context — postcondition base64-urlsafe-confusion added in depth pass. JWT segments and OAuth state tokens use base64url encoding (- and _ instead of + and /); standard isBase64() returns false for these. Detect when isBase64() is called without { urlSafe: true } and the variable/function name suggests JWT or OAuth context.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: isBase64() urlSafe confusion requires context-aware analysis (JWT/OAuth variable names). Too heuristic-based. Not a throw pattern.

description: Add detection for str.length comparison used instead of validator.isLength() — postcondition length-vs-string-length-mismatch added in depth pass. String.prototype.length counts surrogate pairs (emoji) as 2 units; isLength() counts grapheme clusters as 1. When code checks value.length > N as a DB column limit guard without using isLength(), flag as potential surrogate-pair mismatch. Scanner approach: look for .length comparisons adjacent to string validation code in the same function.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: str.length vs isLength() surrogate pair mismatch requires detecting .length comparisons adjacent to validation code. Not a throw pattern. Highly context-dependent.

description: Add detection for validator.isISO8601() called without strict mode — postcondition iso8601-non-strict-invalid-dates added in depth pass. Non-strict mode accepts calendar-invalid dates like '2009-02-29' (Feb 29 in a non-leap year). These pass isISO8601() but are silently coerced by Date.parse() (e.g. to March 1). Detect when isISO8601() is called with no options or without { strict: true }.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: isISO8601() strict mode — detecting absence of { strict: true }. Not a throw pattern (returns false). Would require option-aware analysis on a function that never throws.

description: Add detection for validator.isISO8601() called without strictSeparator option — postcondition iso8601-strict-separator added in depth pass. Without strictSeparator, mixed-separator dates like '2009-01-01T00:00 00:00' pass. Detect when isISO8601() is called with { strict: true } but without { strictSeparator: true } as a lower-severity warning.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

resolution: isISO8601() strictSeparator — same as deepen-6. Returns false, not throws. Not a try-catch pattern.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis, detection rule needed

description: Add detection for socket.io socket.use() per-socket middleware — postcondition added in depth pass (socket-use-middleware-error-no-listener, socket-use-middleware-async-unhandled) but no scanner rule exists for per-socket middleware error listener detection

evidence.note: Proactive — per-socket middleware error patterns require tracking socket.use() calls and verifying socket.on("error") listener presence on same socket instance

resolution: Rejected: Rejected: socket.use() per-socket middleware requires tracking that a socket.on("error") listener is registered on the same socket instance. This requires per-instance event listener correlation across statements — more complex than the current event-listener-absence plugin which checks class-level patterns. Defer to a future cycle.

description: Add detection for socket.io BroadcastOperator.emitWithAck() — postconditions added (broadcast-emit-with-ack-timeout-unhandled, broadcast-emit-with-ack-no-timeout) but scanner needs to detect io.timeout().emitWithAck() and io.to(room).emitWithAck() call chains

evidence.note: Proactive — BroadcastOperator chains via io.to()/io.in()/io.timeout() — requires chained method detection for emitWithAck on BroadcastOperator. Distinct from socket.emitWithAck() already contracted

resolution: Rejected: Rejected: BroadcastOperator.emitWithAck() requires detecting chained calls like io.timeout(ms).emitWithAck() and io.to(room).emitWithAck() — a chained-method pattern on the BroadcastOperator. The property-chain-detector covers linear chains but BroadcastOperator is accessed via multiple entry points (io.to, io.in, io.timeout). Defer to a future cycle when BroadcastOperator chain detection is implemented.

description: Add detection for chai.assert.ifError() — postcondition assert-iferror-rethrows-value added in depth pass. assert.ifError(val) rethrows val directly (not AssertionError), so catch blocks expecting only AssertionError will silently miss errors from assert.ifError. Scanner needs to detect assert.ifError() calls that are either: (1) not in a try-catch at all, or (2) in a try-catch that only catches AssertionError (via instanceof check). The unique behavioral contract is that the thrown error is val itself, not a chai AssertionError.

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis (depth pass 2026-04-20). assert.ifError is the only chai method that does NOT throw AssertionError on failure — it rethrows val. Detection rule needed to catch misuse in production code (try-catch that only catches AssertionError).

resolution: Rejected: Insufficient evidence (violation_count: 0, no real-repo sightings). chai.assert.ifError() is a rarely-used method that rethrows val (not AssertionError). Requires specialized scanner rule to detect misuse. No FP reports from dashboard. Revisit when real-world violations are observed.

description: Add detection for sinon.fake(nonFunction) — postcondition fake-non-function-arg-throws added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Throws TypeError: Expected f argument to be a Function. Source confirmed: sinon/lib/sinon/fake.js lines 30-32

resolution: Rejected: fake-non-function-arg-throws requires detecting non-function argument types at call sites. Static analysis cannot reliably determine if sinon.fake(x) is called with a non-function — the scanner would fire on ALL sinon.fake(x) calls, creating mass FPs for the common sinon.fake(impl) pattern. This postcondition is not statically checkable without type inference. The contract documentation is correct but this cannot be enforced by the scanner.

description: Add detection for sinon.fake.yields()/yieldsAsync() invoked without callback — postcondition fake-yields-missing-callback added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Throws TypeError: Expected last argument to be a function at invocation time.

resolution: Rejected: fake-yields-missing-callback fires when a yields-fake is invoked without a callback as the last argument. This is a runtime argument check — the scanner would need to track every call site where a yields-fake is invoked and verify the last argument is a function. This is not feasible with static analysis of call sites. The contract postcondition documents the behavior correctly but cannot be enforced by the scanner.

description: Add detection for validator.unescape() output flowing directly to HTML template — postcondition unescape-xss-reintroduction added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. High severity: unescape() reverses escape() protection. Output passed to innerHTML/template literals creates XSS vector.

resolution: Rejected: unescape-xss-reintroduction is a security usage pattern postcondition, not a throws/exception postcondition. The scanner checks for try-catch around function calls. It cannot detect whether unescape() output flows to innerHTML or template literals — that would require taint analysis. The postcondition documents an important security concern but the scanner cannot enforce this pattern without dataflow analysis. The contract.yaml documentation is valuable for human review but is not statically checkable.

description: Add detection for validator.blacklist()/whitelist() with dynamic chars argument — postconditions blacklist-regex-injection and whitelist-regex-injection added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. chars param directly interpolated into RegExp without escaping. Dynamic chars from user input or config causes regex injection.

resolution: Rejected: blacklist-regex-injection and whitelist-regex-injection require detecting whether the chars argument is derived from user input — this is taint analysis, not call-site try-catch detection. The scanner cannot determine at static analysis time whether the chars parameter is dynamic or static. The contract postconditions are documented correctly but cannot be enforced by the scanner without dataflow/taint analysis.

description: Add detection for validator.toBoolean() called without strict=true in config/feature-flag contexts — postcondition to-boolean-loose-mode-truthy-surprise added in depth pass

evidence.note: Proactive — contract added via API surface analysis. Non-strict mode (default): toBoolean("no") = true, toBoolean("off") = true, toBoolean("disabled") = true — all counterintuitive.

resolution: Rejected: to-boolean-loose-mode-truthy-surprise is a correctness/semantic issue, not an exception-throwing postcondition. The scanner detects missing try-catch around exception-throwing calls. toBoolean() does not throw — it returns a boolean. This postcondition documents a semantic surprise but the scanner has no mechanism to fire on correct-but-counterintuitive return values. The contract documentation is correct but cannot be enforced by exception-detection analysis.

description: Add detection for validator.trim()/ltrim()/rtrim() called with non-string argument — postcondition trim-non-string-throws added in depth pass but no scanner rule exists

resolution: Implemented: validator.trim() is detected by the ThrowingFunctionDetector. The ContractMatcher fires trim-non-string-throws. Verified: the scanner fires violations for validator.trim() calls without try-catch in existing fixtures. No code changes needed.
